[Luzon/Provincial] Sobrang bothersome and disheartening na UPOU students din mismo ang lowkey naglu-look down sa constituent nila ‘no? O di kaya yung mga pumasa ng UPCAT na sa UPOU nakapasok, tinatakpan nila yung “Open University” part kapag pinopost nila. I’m a sophomore from UPOU. Tulad ng iba, binalak ko rin na mag transfer sa [Metro Manila] or [Luzon/Provincial] after my freshman year at meron din akong mindset dati na “basta makapasok sa UP”. However, I didn’t push through with it kasi I found myself enjoying my program in UPOU as well as the independence and freedom it offers. Dahil sa flexible sched namin dito, may time ako para mag work and makakuha ng experience as early as now which will I believe will help me in the long run. Napamahal na ako sa UPOU at sobrang thankful ko na dito ako nakapasok. Proud din ako kapag sinasabi kong nasa UP Open University ako tuwing may nagtatanong. In my two years here, I met different people. Yung iba naging kaibigan ko pa. Kaso lang yung iba sa kanila, kinakahiya o hindi kayang sabihin na nasa UPOU sila. Kapag may nagtatanong sa kanila, sinasabi nila na taga [Metro Manila] sila o ‘di kaya ay sinasabi nga nilang taga OU sila pero hindi nawawala ang karugtong agad na “sa [Metro Manila] din yun”. May isang beses na nag aaral kami ng kaibigan ko sa cafe tapos may nag approach sa kanya na dati nyang kaklase. Kinumusta sya at tinanong sya kung saan sya nag aaral. Ang sabi nya verbatim ay “sa UP... sa [Metro Manila]” tinignan nya pa ‘ko nang may pag aalinlangan bago sabihin yung [Metro Manila]. Tinanong sya kung anong program nya, at sinabi nya naman na BAMS which is totoo naman dahil yun ang program namin. Kaso lang.. walang ganon sa [Metro Manila]. Magsisinungaling na nga lang eh hindi pa ginalingan. Pasalamat sya dahil hindi alam nung kasama nya na walang BAMS sa [Metro Manila], kundi napahiya pa sya ron. Hay. Ang uncomfortable makita ng eksenang yon. Hindi nalang ako umimik at ayokong basagin trip nya pero deep inside nalulungkot ako. Mga kapwa taga UPOU ko pa mismo ang lowkey mababa ang tingin o kinakahiya ang constituent namin. Ang tagal na naming mga taga UPOU sinusubukan na sirain yung stigma surrounding us. Ang tagal na namin pinapatunayan sa iba na kasing UP lang din kami ng [Metro Manila], [Luzon/Provincial], [Baguio/Benguet], and so on. Kami yung mga taong ayaw na sinasabihan ng “taga UPOU lang”. Kami yung mga tao na ayaw mababa ang tingin samin dahil lang hindi kami taga [Metro Manila], [Luzon/Provincial], o iba pang may campus. Pero at the same time, yung iba rin samin ang mga lowkey minamaliit ang UPOU. Kahit hindi nila sabihin, feeling nila inferior ang UPOU sa ibang UP constituents. Para sakanila, hindi kasing UP ng [Metro Manila]/[Luzon/Provincial]/etc. ang UPOU kahit pantay-pantay lang naman ang pagiging UP ng mga constituent regardless. Makikita mo naman yon sa kung paano nila ikahiya na sabihing taga UPOU sila, o kung paano sila magkunwari na taga [Metro Manila], at iba pa. Hay. Nakakalungkot. -02-07 18:49:53

[Baguio/Benguet] Baks mag desisyon ka nga ng maayos hindi yung mag rereklamo ka ng “Ba’t kasi may event pang ganto” or “Ano ba ‘yan gastos nanaman” pero nagrereklamo pa rin pag walang event sa pharma. Eme ka beh, na para bang end of the world na kung magpapasa ka lang ng signed excuse letter sa OSA edi sabehen mu may emirjenzi or walang dyip, crisis dbaaa (oh sarcism yan). Kung alam nyo naman na ang salitang MANDATORY edi matic excused ka, apaka naman atecko😔 Akala mo talaga hindi Pharmacy teachers naka handle sainyu or if hindi man yan sila pumayag edi yun ilagay nyo sa excuse letter😖 Make it make sense kung san napupunta ung 200? Sige beh bigay ko sayo🤗. Sa tingin mo ba na the STUDENTS that are assigned to be the officers at the organization have the time na mag ayos ng venue? Malamang they’ll hire somebody else to do it, tutulong naman yan sila pag may free time sila eh anuba. The fact that they already explained where the 200 Php is going eh nagrereklamo ka pa ineng? Or hindi ka lang nakinig?. Maybe think from their perspectives as STUDENTS who are already busy with school works, lectures and lab then meron pang organization na inaalala—if you think that’s easy to juggle edi ikaw mag handle. Kung busy ka, mas busy sila. Stop acting like they don’t have their own lives outside the campus. Not to be insensitive for those who think it’s a waste of time to go at the event because it’s Sunday but it happens only once a year, ano ba naman yung mag laan kayo ng time for this event to happen? Like oh mag attendance ka or hintayin mo lang namakuha mo yung pagkain na nang galing sa 200 mo then uwi ka na para magawa mo mga gagawin mo sa buhay mo. It’s only for a DAY, not for a WEEK. Don’t you think it’s selfish na mag pa poll sila then the outcome would be less than they expect? Edi washi pupunta sa event kasi Sunday yan sya magaganap? As I said, magrereklamo pa kayo if walang pa event yung Org (I’m not even associated with the organization, taga ibang course pa nga ako eh naaawa nalang sa mga handlers na pinag bibintangan nyo. [Baguio/Benguet] As an ate from higher years (PolSci here 🙋🏻‍♀️), napapansin ko lang sa mga newer batches ngayon na parang konting inconvenience or hindi nasunod gusto, diretso agad sa freedom wall or rant posts. Gets naman na stressful ang college, lalo na kapag sabay-sabay requirements at hassle sa schedules. Pero part din talaga ng college (and eventually adulthood) yung learning how to adjust, manage time, and handle situations professionally kahit inconvenient. Hindi rin kasi lahat ng offices or tao makakapag-adjust sa personal schedules natin. Minsan kailangan talaga tayo yung gagawa ng paraan, kahit hassle. Ganun din sa real world after graduation.. deadlines, documents, offices, work schedules, hindi laging convenient. Parang masyado nang normalized ngayon na lahat nalang ipo-post or ikukuha ng sympathy online instead of finding solutions first or taking responsibility as growing adults. Hindi naman lahat ng “strict” or “firm” na sagot ibig sabihin masama agad yung tao. Constructive criticism lang from an ate 🥲 Toughen up a little. College is supposed to prepare us for the real world too. May 1, 1:52:27 PM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] An open letter to the girl from engineering I don’t know if this letter will ever reach you in the way I intend, but I needed to write it anyway. There are things I should have said before, and things I wish I had understood sooner. Most of all, I’m sorry. Sorry for the ways I hurt you whether I realized it at the time or not. Sorry for the moments I chose silence when I should have spoken, distance when I should have stayed, and pride when I should have been gentle. I see now how my actions, and sometimes my inaction, created wounds that didn’t deserve to be there. Regret has a quiet way of staying with you. It shows up in the small hours, in the memories that replay differently when you finally understand what they meant. I’ve had time to reflect, and in that reflection, I see more clearly the weight of what I lost—not just the relationship, but the trust, the warmth, and the version of us that could have been. You deserved better from me. That truth sits heavily, but it’s one I accept fully. I’m not writing this to reopen anything or to ask for another chance. Some things, once broken, aren’t meant to be rebuilt and I respect that. I’m writing because you deserved an apology that didn’t come too late or too quietly. I hope you’ve found peace. The kind that isn’t interrupted by old memories or unanswered questions. I hope your days are lighter now, filled with people and moments that treat your heart with the care it always deserved. And if my absence has brought you that peace, then I accept that too. You mattered to me more than I showed, more than I knew how to express at the time. That won’t change, even as life moves us further apart. Take care of yourself. Truly. May 1, 7:12:03 AM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] Cheating Issues sa ESELYU I don’t really get the policy na need pa ng evidence para macontinue ung pagreport sa mga nagchecheat. Its been weeks since midterms exams, prelims or since from the 1st semester na andaming rumors ng cheating pero wala paring nagagawa kasi wala namang evidence na naipapasa. Is [Baguio/Benguet] creating and tolerating future criminals? Hindi ba enough what most of the class have seen during the exam, quizzes? Buong klase na o, hindi ba evidence yoon since we’re in the same block, we know kung ano ang nangyayari sa loob ng classroom. And honestly, it’s exhausting. Kasi hindi lang ito tungkol sa “rules” or “policies”. it’s about fairness. Paano magiging fair yung grading system kung may mga students na nagpapakahirap mag-aral, nagpupuyat, sumusunod sa rules, habang yung iba nagche-cheat (e.g gumagamit ng phone during exam, may maliit na paper as kodigo) lang pero same or even higher pa yung nakukuhang grades? It’s not just nakakainis, it’s discouraging. Nakakawala ng gana. Kasi parang anong point pa ng integrity kung sa system mismo hindi nakikita yun? We’re told over and over na mahalaga ang honesty, na core value siya, pero sa actual na nangyayari, parang selective lang ang enforcement. Ang dali sabihin ng “we uphold integrity” during exam weeks pero saan yun kapag actual na may naging case ng cheating and kailangan nang kumilos? And what’s worse is still seeing those same people act proud na “ uy para bang walang nangyari.” Yung tipong sila pa yung may “honesty and integrity,” raw kahit alam ng buong klase kung ano yung ginagawa nila. Pero kapag tatanungin mo during recitations and reportings wala namang naisasagot. Nakaka-disrespect po siya noh, siya sa mga taong pinipiling maging honest and truthful sa pagaaral nila. Hindi rin naman fair na laging burden of proof nasa students. We’re not investigators. We’re not there to secretly record or gather evidence during exams. students din kami na dapat focused sa sarili naming ginagawa. Need namin magcontinue sa pag rereview after a course (in our schedule ganito). If multiple people are already raising the same concern, hindi ba dapat may mas proactive na action from the faculty? Kahit man lang investigation, stricter monitoring, or changes sa exam setup? Because right now, ang nangyayari is simple: those who cheat get rewarded, get praised, and those who don’t, get left questioning kung worth it pa bang maging honest. nako nako eselyu, what is happening to you? May 1, 5:30:09 AM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] Share ko lang yung experience namin earlier sa Registrar Window 10. Maayos naman kami nakipag-usap from the start, pero hindi talaga okay yung naging approach sa amin. Kailangan kasi naming mag-submit ng document, and nakalagay sa portal “at our convenient time.” Pero ang situation namin, nasa Bakakeng Campus kami and kailangan pa pumunta sa main campus para lang mag-submit. Eh whole day pa classes namin from Monday to Saturday, so sobrang hirap talaga maghanap ng time para dito. Tapos sinabi pa sa amin na until Wednesday next week yung deadline. Eh online classes naman ngayon, so maraming students ang uuwi sa province nila—including us. So lalo siyang naging hassle sa side namin. Nakiusap naman kami nang maayos kung pwede after online class na lang kami mag-submit, since matagal din i-process yung document. Pero ang sagot sa amin, “Hindi, kailangan niyo gawan ng paraan.” Honestly, nakaka-disappoint lang. Hindi naman kami nagrereklamo for no reason—nag-eexplain lang kami ng situation namin. Sana kahit konting consideration man lang sa students. Sana rin in cases like this, pwede na lang i-email yung documents tapos may clear na deadline. Mas madali sana para sa lahat. And sana mas maayos yung pakikitungo sa students—kasi maayos naman kaming lumapit in the first place. [REDACTED_NAME] 30, 7:11:59 PM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] Honest Review ng [Baguio/Benguet] Ladies' Dorm na tumira ng buong college life doon. Pros: - malapit sa school, convenient especially kapag may mga 7:30 AM classes ka. Tipid ka pa sa pamasahe and di ka pa bibiyahe. Kahit 6:30 ka pa gumising, keri lang. - Safe, recommended siya for freshmen na hindi pa alam ang pasikot sikot sa Baguio. Since within the school yung dorm, talagang safe yung place. - Food: Honestly, okay naman ang food doon. Minsan may mga questionable, pero may mga masasarap din. Usually, may main na 1 or 2 na ulam yon. Minsan may soup/fruits/desserts na kasama. Laging may Milo sa morning, kumuha ka nalang. Convenient kasi di ka na magluluto. If gusto mo na ikaw yung magluto, nag-ooffer ang [Baguio/Benguet] LRH ng bed spacers. If ayaw mo talaga yung food, malapit ang [Baguio/Benguet] sa fastfood resto. May newly opened din na kainan sa parking lot ng Sacred Heart. - Laundry Services: May dalawang laundry services na pumupunta sa dorm except Sunday. If may mga need kayong iparush na uniforms or ano pa man, pwede magrequest no extra charge (sa "B" 'to guys - verify niyo pa'rin, not sure sa isang laundry service kung nagpaparush sila). Sila na magpipick-up at magdedeliver, since may designated schedule lang sila na pwedeng pumunta. Kung ayaw mo magpalaba, may laundry area sa 2nd floor. May mga common na sampayan don, pero meron ding mga personal. - Events: Every semester may two to three events ang dorm. Kapag 1st sem, acquiantance party, pajama Party, movie night, may mga eme eme din kapag bago exam. Naranasan ko rin yung nagbigay sila ng chocolates with matching motivational short message na nakalagay noon sa card (?). Dati may sports fest din. Pabago bago to guys, nakadepende yung events sa gusto ng dorm and ng officers. These events usually ay ginaganap after the exams or before short vacations. - Rooms: Nagooffer sila ng mga rooms depende sa needs mo. Afaik, may solo, double, quad (loft type), quad (single bed), not sure kung may pang walo (either board and lodging or bed spacer). With own cr, shower room, sink, except yung sa mga pang walo na mga rooms (common cr and shower), and study tables, cabinets. - Generator: walang power interruption sa LRH kasi sa generator ng school. CONS: - Rules: 7PM curfew, if need na lumagpas doon may mga leave pass sila na need ipapirma (hassle, especially kapag biglaan yung need mong gawin or yung labas niyo), study periods weekdays. Exempted kapag DL, pero kapag exam week suspended yung Optional Study Period and Leave Passes. - Study Period: 7:45 PM to 9:15 PM, if ikaw yung klase ng tao na need magsalita ng malakas para magreview o kaya lakad ng lakad kapag nag-aaral, madaling madistract. Baka di ka makapag-aral ng maayos here. Ang study period ng LRH kasi ipagsasama kayo sa study hall, maluwang naman yon. Pero kapag 1st sem tapos hindi pa nalalabas yung DL usually tatlo kayo sa isang long table so medyo masikip. Marami ring tayo ng tayo kapag study period kaya madidistract ka kasi minsan ramdam mo sa floor yung paggalaw. Every study period may opening and closing prayer yon, so kapag late nagstart yung study period niyo, late din kayo matatapos. Kapag din, may bigla biglang maingay or may mga phone nagriring/alarm, extra time sa study yon ng buong dorm (so damay damay kayo). Dito usually maraming nagkakaroon ng violations, late dumadating sa study period, absent, or sunod sunod yung pagbibigay mo ng excuse letter para sa study period. - Food: may time lang ang food. Example, breakfast 6 to 7:30 or 7:45 ata. Kapag late kayo nagising (wag naman super late, siguro mga 8:30 or 9) open pa naman ang kitchen. Pero depende na yon kung may natira pang food nung breakfast. Pwede kayong kumain, pero kapag lagpas na kayo sa time ng pagkain, kayo na maghuhugas ng plato niyo. Sa Lunch, 11:00 to 12:30 ata, pero usually may pagkain pa naman ng hanggang 1:30. Sa dinner, 6:00 to 7:30, hanggang 7:45 lang dito kasi naglilinis na yung mga kitchen staff and uuwi rin sila. Sooo, kung super duper late pa rin diyan yung uwi ninyo, maglunch box nalang kayo (make sure na may food stub kayo, need ulit pirma) - Roommates: Swertehan lang 'to guys, minsan kasi may mga roommates talaga na parang walang pake or inconsiderate (?). Meron yung ang lakas lakas manood sa phone or may kacall, tawa ng tawa ng malakas, meron yung hindi gumagawa ng chores niya (kayo rin kasi maglilinis ng kwarto niyo, gawa nalang kayo ng sched), meron ding mga rooms from other floors na super ang tawa, hiyawan, kantahan and ewan kahit lagpas na ng silence period (10 PM). Nasa sa inyo nalang yan kung paano niyo iaaddress sa isa't isa yung mga issues niyo. - Forms: actually, ito yung pinakahassle talaga for me. Parang bawat galaw mo, forms. Uuwi ka sa baba, form. Lunch box, pirma. Late uuwi, pirma. Kukuha ng food delivery ng gabi, pirma. Late nakarating ng dorm, pirma. Kada gabi, attendance. Gets ko naman na for monitoring and safety purposes yon, medyo hassle lang talaga lalo na kapag pagod ka na or puyat. - Own things: Minsan may mga instances na may mawawala nalang na food mo sa ref, especially kapag sa bed spacer ka. Minsan ganun din sa laundry sa 2nd floor. - Violations: dito na medyo nagkakagulo, minsan kasi talaga inconsistent yung rules ng dorm when it comes sa implementation. So basta kapag marami na kayong violations, either di ka na pwede sa dorm or may conditions ka na next sem (ex. no leave pass for 1 month). - Indepedent Living: hindi ka matututo mamuhay ng independent dito. Provided na ang pagkain mo, ibibigay mo nalang laundry mo, di ka na lalabas ng school, basically nakatira ka sa school, so paranh hindi ka talaga matuto kung paano mamuhay ng indepedent kasi super provided na almost everything sa dorm. - Wifi: gosh, grabe ang wifi dito guys. Lalo na kapag start ng study period. So I suggest bumili kayo ng pocket wifi or personal router niyo para hindi hassle. Also, banned ang Tiktok sa wifi ng LRH, magVPN ka nalang. Dati pati IG and Shopee pero ngayon okay naman na. - Events: may mga events na mandatory andun kayo. Which is for me minsan ay hassle, lalo na kapag galing kang duty, gusto mo nalang magpahinga or kung may need ka ng tapusin na school works. - If bago ka lang sa Baguio tapos ikaw yung klase ng tao na aral lang talaga or may strict na parents, I suggest dito. Pero sa totoo lang, if kahit papaano alam mo sa sarili mo na responsable ka namang tao and gusto mo ng freedom habang nag-aaral pa rin, wag here. [REDACTED_NAME] 29, 11:26:19 PM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] Ang hirap lumaban ng patas sa [Baguio/Benguet]. Instead of fostering fairness, some policies appear to result in shared consequences where even those uninvolved in wrongdoing are affected. In such cases, students who act with integrity are still affected in the same way, raising serious concerns about fairness. This reflects a deeper issue that continues to weaken students’ trust in the system. When students raise valid concerns, these are often dismissed or turned back on them. Those who speak out are met with further difficulty. Assessments become more challenging, and the academic environment grows more hostile than responsive. Now, concerns are also being raised regarding the fairness of examinations themselves. Questions persist as to whether uniform standards were upheld and whether all students were assesses under equivalent conditions. If the measures used to evaluate learning are themselves called into question, it becomes difficult to expect students to maintain trust in the system. This is not simply a complaint, but a call to uphold the standards and values the institution claims to stand for. [REDACTED_NAME] 25, 11:22:55 PM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] Kung usapang fairness, dapat malinaw. Hindi porket ganun ang exam namin, ibig sabihin sobrang dali na agad. [Baguio/Benguet] Message to Teachers on Online Classes (SONAHBS) Online classes are meant to help us adjust, but they're starting to feel overwhelming. I’m writing this in hopes that we can make better use of the time in this setup. Instead of adding extra activities that wouldn’t normally be given in a face-to-face class, it might be more effective if we were given this time for actual studying (through discussions or asynchronous reading assignments ). I understand that our educational system in the Philippines is very output-based, but we already have a heavy enough workload even in the regular full face-to-face setup. Adding more tasks on top of that (during the OLC) leaves little time to actually understand and review the material (speaking as a frustrated learner who needs more time to study). At times, it feels like the online period is not being used as intentionally as it could be, and it ends up becoming counterproductive. I also know that this shift has been sudden and challenging for teachers as well. But at the same time, it’s frustrating when time and effort are spent on activities that don’t meaningfully contribute to learning. Not all courses benefit from more written outputs. Many of our subjects require us to spend long periods studying, understanding, and memorizing the material, and we need time for that. This is not for everyone, as some instructors have been able to use the time well, and put real effort into teaching the material. I really appreciate that. I just hope this reaches the right audience who may actually need to rethink their approach. We are all in a difficult situation, and we hope that our teachers can be more careful in deciding what will truly help the students, rather than simply assigning tasks for the sake of having something to fill in the time. [REDACTED_NAME] 22, 11:58:21 AM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] Adults in Age, Not in Conflict You’re an adult. You witness a colleague handle a concern the right way—privately, directly, and with the appropriate people involved. Then, unexpectedly, someone else chooses a different route. Instead of addressing the matter where it belongs, they involve a “protector” and shift the conversation into something public, indirect, and quietly hostile. You start to wonder—what exactly is this behavior? If you’ve experienced this, take note: your instinct isn’t misplaced. What you’re seeing isn’t really about resolution. It’s a pattern. At its core, it often reflects a conflict style built on avoidance and image protection. Some individuals struggle with direct, uncomfortable conversations. Rather than engaging head-on, they navigate conflict in ways that preserve how they are perceived, even if it complicates the situation. What tends to drive this? Emotional immaturity. Not everyone develops the same level of emotional regulation, accountability, or direct problem-solving. Age alone doesn’t guarantee growth—intentional effort does. Avoidance of confrontation. Discomfort with being challenged, uncertainty in expressing their side, or fear of vulnerability can lead people to take indirect routes instead of addressing issues directly. Image management. Maintaining the appearance of being “right” or “wronged” can become more important than resolving the issue itself. So the narrative gets shaped early—often before the full story is even heard. Power over communication. Bringing in a third party shifts the dynamic. Instead of a balanced conversation, it becomes a situation of leverage. This is less about dialogue and more about advantage. Learned patterns. For some, this isn’t new behavior. It may stem from environments where indirect handling of conflict was normalized—or even rewarded. Indirect expression. Frustrations don’t disappear; they simply surface in subtler ways—side comments, selective storytelling, or passive-aggressive remarks. In essence: Avoidance + insecurity + narrative control = indirect conflict. And that’s the key point—this isn’t someone seeking resolution. It’s someone seeking comfort, validation, or control. So when a calm, straightforward conversation doesn’t work, it’s not necessarily a failure on your part. It’s a mismatch in approach. To wrap things up—karma has a way of arriving right on time. I only wish I had front-row seats when it does. Sending this off with nothing but class and composure. [REDACTED_NAME] 22, 11:29:39 AM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] Speaking from both sides. As a graduate of [Baguio/Benguet] who experienced both the old and new administration, nothing will happen if you guys will exchange rants dahil lang sa hindi tayo pareho ng pinagdaanan. We experienced the flooding of 7th floor ng perfecto kasi tumutulo tubig sa bubong dahil sa ulan. The Elevator of Perfecto na halos buong buhay ko sa [Baguio/Benguet] ay sira, although natry din naman naming magamit. The Library na kailangan mong maghagdan kasi out of order ang elevator, naexperienced din namin yun. Yung ground floor at 2nd floor ng STELA na kala mo kung kulungan at sa 3rd floor na maasim. Sa Aden, mabaho. CR walang tubig, magiigib ka sa drum, ngayon maayos na. Yung silang dati, para kang pumasok sa kulungan ng manok sa sobrang baba ng headroom, ngayon maganda na ang facilities. Yung Rizal Building, maayos na din elevator at hindi na masyadong amoy formaline. Marami ng nagbago, and most of it naranasan din namin. Isa din kami sa mga nag rally ng against TOFI, nagstart ata yan nung nawalan ng 1st years and 2nd years dahil sa Senior High Curriculum. So around or pero before yun, may increase na din hindi nga lang ganun kalaki kasi mura pa naman ang mga bilihin nun. And yung increase na yun ang dahilan kung bakit may mga bago kayong tables and chairs, working elevator, renovated buildings and other facilities, so I don't think walang say ang mga alumni dito. Kung pag-uusapan naman ang strength, both naman magkaiba ang lakas, bago na ang curriculum ninyo, so I don't think we could compare, although yes, siguro masasabi nating hasa sa manual ang mga alumni kasi hindi pa kilala ang AI na karamihan sa inyong new students, dun nakadepende. To where we stand before, you really have to stay late at night para magbasa, unlike now na isang click mo lang may sagot na. Kung sinasabi mong hindi affected ang alumni kasi hindi nila nararamdaman yun, well they do feel it, hindi lang ninyo nakikita kasi hindi naman karamihan sa inyo na new students ay part nun. One of the benefactors ng [Baguio/Benguet] ay ang mga Alumni, and may mga scholars sila na nag-aaral sa [Baguio/Benguet]. Ito yung mga students na gusto makapagtapos ngunit hindi ganun kaganda ang buhay. Not to mention those anak ng mga alumni na nagaaral sa [Baguio/Benguet], so affected pa rin sila. And malaking bagay yun for both parties kasi nakatutulong ang mga alumni natin sa mga gusto makapag-aral and at the same time, may funding ang schools sa ibang activities gaya ng mga extension activities ng univ. Non-Gov't kasi si [Baguio/Benguet] kaya san sila kukuha ng pondo para mameet ang demands ng students? Yes hindi ganung kalaki yung nababago, pero may nagbago. Sa mga alumni naman, huwag din nating i-invalidate yung nararamdaman ng mga adings natin. iba na kasi sila ngayon, ang mga batang ito ay hindi sanay sa mga matitinding bakbakan. Magkaiba na ang mundo natin, although nararanasan parin naman natin yun, kaso sa labas na ng univ. Ibang problema na ang kaharap natin. Hayaan natin nilang maranasan yung hirap sa loob para hindi sila mabigla kung gaano kahirap sa labas. Na hindi lang pangalan ng [Baguio/Benguet] ang dala mo, kundi ang Tatak ng pagiging Tunay na Louisyano 💪🏻 [REDACTED_NAME] 19, 10:57:41 PM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] "I'm stuck here but I might ask for the bill sooner than I expected." I've been in this restaurant before. It was beautiful and full of warmth. The first time, it was very nice. Not the greatest menu but I was content with everything. I mean, I was being served well. But that was just in the beginning. The menu became stale as time went by, and the bountiful feast turned into crumbs. But I was down deep already that I still found myself settling for those crumbs. Because I thought, maybe this restaurant just lacked the ingredients for now. But I was wrong. It went on for long that I just decided to leave. But of course, I regretted it. Months later, the restaurant welcomed me again. On the outside, it seemed to have been renovated. I was served better and the menu was great. I was happy again and thought, maybe this time is really 'the moment'. But it was really just that - a moment, for it didn't last long. Same old thing happened again. The restaurant just couldn't keep up with what it used to be at the beginning. I found myself settling for crumbs again. This time, however, was more painful. I chose to stay in this restaurant because I had hoped that maybe it just needed some time to grow. But in the middle of waiting, the lights went out. I knew everything was not right already but to completely shut off without warning - I... I'm just left here wondering what I was to you after all this time. What my purpose or my place here was. Because I truly believed that I belonged here. You made me. I tried calling for your name but to no avail, my voice just echoed through the desolate corners of this restaurant. In this darkness, I was left here to wait alone. I'm just tired of it all. Because even after all the things you did, I still chose you. But you couldn't even bring yourself to face me and tell me the very thing I just needed to hear from you. You turn avoidant when things get serious. And then you try to pull back whenever I took a step back. It's a cycle and I'm always at the losing end. I'm stuck here like a ghost that haunts your walls but I may choose to leave for good now. Maybe one of these days, I'll finally ask for the bill. But I'll just stay a little longer. I miss you but I miss myself more now. If we are really meant for one another, maybe we'll find each other again - when you can finally meet me in between. - 🎨 [REDACTED_NAME] 19, 12:29:44 PM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] Bridging Generations. . . We stand today at the meeting point of two worlds—the world that shaped us, and the world shaping the youth. Our generation grew up in libraries, turning pages with patience, searching for wisdom line by line. The younger generation grows up with the internet, where answers appear in seconds, and life moves at lightning speed. It is easy to compare, but comparison is not the path forward. The truth is, they are not weaker for living differently. They are stronger for adapting to the world they were given. Their resilience lies in coping with rapid change, just as ours lay in resourcefulness and persistence. Our role as the older generation is not to judge, but to guide. To meet them halfway. To listen, to understand, and to pass on the values that never fade—respect, kindness, perseverance, accountability and responsibility. These are the bridges that connect us, no matter how wide the gap may seem. Generations may differ in tools, but not in humanity. The internet may replace the library, but it cannot replace the values that make us whole. Let us stop measuring the youth against the past, and instead embrace them as the future. Together, let us build a world where wisdom and innovation walk side by side. Where the strength of yesterday meets our youth today. Let’s hear them. Let’s understand them. Let’s learn from them. [REDACTED_NAME] 18, 9:11:34 PM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] A messy rant from someone who's tired af but keeps going for myself: It's true na maraming resources ngayon at easily accessible because of the internet, mabilis na google search lng may sagot ka na, mas lalo na at may AI na rin (altho I hate generative AI and I refuse to use it, I'll submit a low quality essay than use AI, that's MY principle so no offense). But if we reallyyy think about it, are these resources even factual? Also, many are behind pay walls and for ME I don't want to voluntarily give ANY personal information on an online website even if it's secured, plus following the syllabus, if the prof even gives a syllabus, isn't even guarantee coz the references listed are sometimes not easily accessible genuinely. And it's so easy to spread misinformation because of how easily accessible almost everything is. Just because there's many resources doesn't mean it's useful. Some topics are HARD to find. This is coming from someone who lost sleeping searching all over the internet finding references that are factual, peer reviewed, and up to date (so many papers r so old that it doesn't fit the given criteria). And WHAT resources are we gonna use? The time dedicated to other equally/more important things is gonna be eaten up by searching the materials needed. So stop using "there's so many resources nowadays" as an excuse to INVALIDATE the students who are SPEAKING UP coz YOU COULDN'T. We're the ones who has to fix the old gens negligence just so we could LIVE in a world where you don't have to be in constant survival mode, the least you can do is be open minded about change, did history not teach you that society is dynamic? To have the young gens lesser insignificant hardships coz 'bata ang pag-asa ng bayan'? (I hate that phrase, I get the thought but now it's just giving us problems to solve that shouldn't even be a problem in the first place if ppl matured) I'm not angry, I'm just disappointed that the generations that should've matured and teach wisdom are the ones who are against us. So no, we don't have it easy, nor the next generations will have it easy, times change, keep up and break the cycle. And why tf are you bashing someone younger than you? Please reflect on yourself, genuinely. There's so much more to talk abt but then I'd reach the character limit. TL;DR: Your suffering from before doesn't give you the right to invalidate others who's in a different time and place than you. [REDACTED_NAME] 18, 8:45:36 PM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] Somehow I struggle to remember any achievements I've made in my life. I can't remember anything off the top of my head, I have to struggle to remember even one. And even when I do, it feels pointless. Meaningless. Like none of it will ever be enough. Because I wasn't doing it for myself, was I? I was doing it for people who wanted to see me do it. It was all I ever heard. Every diploma. Every medal. Every fancy piece of paper that said I was the best. I hoped that they would make me worth loving, because that was the only way I knew how to earn that. Yet every mishap between or after only reminded me that I am worth less than the dirt I tread upon. I am not worth loving if I am not performing at my best. I am not worth sparing an afterthought. I am nothing more than a statistic. A report card. A dusty medal hanging from a picture frame. If any of that is even worth remembering. [REDACTED_NAME] 18, 5:01:27 PM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] Enroll lang kayo! Nagsimula ako sa [Baguio/Benguet] na may high hopes, pero pagka pasok, andaming issues sa facilities, guro na di nagtuturo, mga terror na prof na minsan feeling mo namamahiya, may pasok pag bagyo at school activities na sinasabi niyo. Totoo din na may flaws talaga ang administration, pero sa [Baguio/Benguet] ako hinubog para sa tunay na mundo. Sabi mo other school teaches their students to be leaders while [Baguio/Benguet] teaches you to be workers? Reassess the statement. 1. Student Activism- buhay na buhay ang student participation ngayon compared before. Dati, we were taught na mali yan but look at how involved [Baguio/Benguet] now, students are not labelled but encouraged to challenge the existing system. 2. Facilities- we studied in a dense room, pangit na upuan, minsan may leaks sa roof, walang elevator etc. [Baguio/Benguet] now has one of best facilities in the region. Aquatics issue? Idulog niyo yan sa administration properly. TBH, [Baguio/Benguet] taught us to follow basic professional systems in place... ang last call to action is public outcry. 3. Professors/ Teachers- you name them. Varying talaga mga Ito, may kinasuklaman kami, may kinatakutan, at may minahal. Pero sa real world, yung mga prof kung Kelan kami naghirap? Yan yung pinakatumatak Ang tinuro at applicable sa real world situation. I’m not invalidating your concerns, but that makes [Baguio/Benguet] as [Baguio/Benguet]. Hinuhubog tayo sa real world situation. Sa totoong buhay, no one cares if you have least or more of the available resources, may mamemeet kang masusungit at mababait. Sa court rooms or sa negotiation table, labanan at tagisan yan and [Baguio/Benguet] taught me to be firm and diplomatic. I usually leave the room with a positive result, if failed ako now, napanalo ko yung sumunod. If you think that the world will go for you and it’s a comfortable life, wag ka mag enroll sa [Baguio/Benguet]. Pero kapag gusto mo ng wide professional network, makilala ang mga supervisors at agency leaders na Louisiano, at pride kapag sinabi mong galing ka [Baguio/Benguet] ka ay hindi ka ididisrespect pero may expectations na you have something to contribute, MAG ENROL KA! [Baguio/Benguet] is only for the brave and hopeful, it is not made for the weak. OO madaling pumasok at mahirap lumabas at OO nag struggle ako sa mental health at kulang sa emotional support, pero dito ako hinubog para maging better over my work peers. Sa totoo lang, Big 4 Universities does not complain kung hindi tinuro ng prof nila ang exam, ang mindset nila is “Kelangan abutin ang standard ni Prof para pumasa.”- hindi sila spoon feeding. Kung may edge sila sa [Baguio/Benguet]? Yan yun. Kinaya nilang aralin ang trabaho sa independent research ng policy and systems, yan ang inaral ko na diko narealise na tinuturo ng mga terror na prof ko noon. Catholic education teaches you to be compassionate, but it trains you firmly. That’s discipline. Mag enrol lang sa [Baguio/Benguet] kung gusto niyong mahubog para sa totoong laban sa buhay. Iwasan mo ang [Baguio/Benguet] kung gusto mo ng comfortable life, hindi yan ituturo sa Louisian. Sa mga student na nahihirapan at nagdadalawang isip kung ano ang pinasok nila sa buhay, ituloy niyo yan!! Hindi niyo lang alam gano kami ka proud kapag dinadagdagan niyo Ang roster ng mga professionals sa Pilipinas at Mundo. - from nag 7 years sa [Baguio/Benguet], 3 times nag shift, multiple times na bumagsak, pero dirediretso ang promotion sa work. [Baguio/Benguet] Let’s hear it from an Alumni. I have been with [Baguio/Benguet] since birth until I graduated from college, and now my child is also with [Baguio/Benguet], taking up a pre-med course. During RetDem days, professors gave clear instructions—guidelines, dos and don’ts were emphasized. Yet, there was one incident where a student failed to properly dispose materials used in their lab activity. The prof asked the class to identify who was responsible, but no one admitted to the mistake. As a result, the prof announced that the entire class would receive a deduction unless the accountable student spoke up. This situation created anxiety among students. They worked hard to earn decent scores, but because of one person’s mistake, everyone’s efforts were penalized. While accountability is indeed a vital value—especially for future professionals who will handle lives—it should be taught in ways that do not unfairly punish others. For example, accountability could be reinforced through practical measures such as: Providing designated trash bins for each group or Using color-coded materials to trace responsibility more easily. In the real world, mistakes are handled through due process. Investigations are conducted before sanctions are given, ensuring that the right person is held accountable. In this case, the sanction relied on the hope that the violator would admit the mistake, but when that doesn’t happen, the entire class suffers. I respectfully urge the institution to adopt fairer and more just approaches in handling such situations. [Baguio/Benguet] has been an esteemed institution for decades, and surely there are established guidelines for addressing these scenarios. Please protect the integrity of the students’ hard work—they should not have to bear the consequences of another’s defiance. [REDACTED_NAME] 18, 7:26:47 AM HKT

I respectfully urge you to strengthen your screening process and be more mindful of the content being approved for posting. Dumoble kasi yung presyo ng rent namin here sa boarding, from ₱3,*** to ₱7,*** bedspacer lang din ako and sobrang nalula ako sa itinaas. Hindi ko alam kung dahil ba super lapit ng location sa univ or gawa lang ng “negosyo”. Nagswitch kami ng landlord kelan lang, tho nag heads up naman sila noon about sa increase, pero lately lang sinabi yung specific amount, and magrerequire ulit ng advance and deposit. As in halos dumoble, gets naman naman na may mga adjustments talaga sa presyo knowing na may crisis, pero nakakabigla talaga. Nagsearch ako sa internet for awareness lang. RA Rent Control Act, may allowed annual increase lang regardless sa pagswitch ng landlord, like 1-3% lang dapat ang itataas. Kaya napaisip lang po ako kung tama ba yung ganitong kalaking adjustments. As much as I want na magstay here hanggang boards, hindi na kakayanin ng budget. Hindi naman pinapaalis agad, and nagbigay naman ng deadline pero hindi rin secured na may lilipatan agad kasi busy pa and hassle din maglipat ng gamit kasi on going pa OJT. [Baguio/Benguet] This is for all incoming freshmen na iisipin if mag eenrol pa ba sila, students that share the same burdens w/ me, and students & mga alumni na masyadong ginaglaze ang [Baguio/Benguet]: GENERAL ISSUES ABT [Baguio/Benguet]: Super pumapanget na sistema nila. Gawad being online or potentially cancelled, sudden shifts sa dry run sa mga events (artsfest), and other things about new shifts sa policies by OSA (i could name more pero sobrang haba na neto). TRENDING ISSUES NI [Baguio/Benguet]: Firstly, noticing that only the administration moves when pumapatok na yung issue. Remember academic break now, hindi niyo binigyan break ang students noong peak to post ng pandemic? Umaksyon lang kayo nung nagtrending na siya nationwide. Nung may mga students na nag aattempt due to stress & overload, nung pumatok din lang to saka kayo nagbigay ng “health and wellness break.” The hair color issue, nung pumatok lang ulit siya saka doon niyo pinayagan na magkaroon ng ibang hair color na hindi natural, tapos nung nawala na nagback to normal kayo. The recent issue about transportation, gumagalaw lang kayo if papatok na siya. There are more hidden issues that your future students do not know, but because you keep on pointing out your “quality education” maraming nasscam. [Baguio/Benguet] FACILITIES: Another one is the facilities. Ano, yung wifi? Most professors expect us to deliver something within the specified period pero paano namin gagawin yun kung mabagal connection at minsan nawawala pa? Other than that, sa mga wifi sa ibang building, binabayaran namin yan pero pahirapan pa makakuha ng password. [Baguio/Benguet] AQUATICS: Isa pa, sa aquatics, although naglay low na to, I just want to point it out. Having basketball & volleyball as an alternative kasi di pa naaayos yung aquatic facility lowers down the competence and resiliency of the university. Estudyante mag aadjust para sa kapalpakan ng preparation sa facility? EXTRACURRICULARS ON [Baguio/Benguet]: University week nagbibigay deadline, intrams may pasok parin, artsfest may pasok parin, and any other major events ng school papasok parin. [Baguio/Benguet] "45 MINUTES" STORY TIME!!! We had this professor who came in midterm, replacing our original instructor. At first we were trying to understand her since bagong adjust pa siya sa class and system. Even during the first meeting, she seemed unsure at times, asking if tapos na ba yung time or clarifying things on the spot, so we really thought she was still settling in. What also made us expect a bit more structure was her background—PhD holder. So naturally, we expected that things would be clear and organized. Before the activity, she mentioned na “over fifty” yung overall score, so yun yung expectation namin going in. Then second meeting, biglang essay agad. Five questions, copy the question, then intro body conclusion each one, all within forty-five minutes. No proper discussion yet, no clear breakdown of how we were supposed to be graded, just go. So we adjusted based on what made sense at that time. May mga sumubok sagutin lahat ng five items kahit maikli na mga sagot, may iba naman na natutukan lang mga few items pero mas complete yung sagot dahil di kinaya ng time. Everyone just did their best with the time and info given. When the papers were returned, that’s when everything got confusing. Most of us got really low scores, around twenty to forty out of one hundred, which didn’t match what we expected at all. Then suddenly sinabi na it’s actually over one hundred pala because there’s a rubric and naadjust din scores. But that rubric was only shown after naibigay yung scores sa next meeting. Ginawa raw na 100 because it supposedly had to follow the high CS requirement, but it ended up being just one quiz that carried an extremely heavy weight on our grades. Wala na kasing other meetings dahil nawalan kami ng sessions noong mga previous meetings bago siya pumalit (walang pumasok kasi inaantay siya). Usually, ang ginagawa sa ibang classes, dinidivide yung CS into multiple quizzes para may chance makabawi—pero dito wala. So basically, the rules kept shifting after we already took the test. Before the quiz we were even told to be concise and comprehensive (yun lang alam namin), but then we found out may rubric pala with five categories per question—content, organization, grammar, and two others—each supposedly worth four points. Pero ang nangyari, yung scores namin per question were around five points total lang, meaning sa bawat category, parang one point lang kami. Halos pantay-pantay lang yung bagsak ng points. She even told us that some of our answers were just repeating the questions, but we were required to write with an introduction, body, and conclusion—so there wasn’t really enough time to think deeply anymore. Honestly, if we were just allowed to answer more directly, it probably would’ve been clearer and better. It’s just hard to understand what exactly the grading was based on, because from our end, we followed the basic structure that was required. We felt na very inconsiderate niya talaga, to the point na parang ang baba ng naging tingin sa outputs namin kahit nag-effort naman kami at sinunod namin yung instructions na binigay at that moment. GANUN PO BA KAMI KAHINA PARA GANUN LANG YUNG SCORES? ANO PO BA YUNG PINAPAHIWATIG? PARANG BINABRAG NIYA PO YUNG PHD NIYA IN A WAY NA DAPAT GANUN KAMI MAGSULAT, EH NABIGLA NGA KAMI. SABI PA DAPAT DAW AYUSIN NAMIN, EH HINDI NAMAN KAMI COMMUNICATION ARTS MAJOR—BSMLS KAMI. SINABI PA NA DAPAT TUMANGGAP KAMI NG PAGKAKAMALI, PERO PARANG ANG HIRAP DIN TANGGAPIN KUNG HINDI NAMAN MALINAW YUNG EXPECTATIONS FROM THE START. PARANG BALIKTAD PA YUNG NANGYAYARI—KAMI PA YUNG PINAPAKITA NA MALI KAHIT NAG-ADJUST NAMAN KAMI BASED ON WHAT WAS GIVEN AT THAT TIME. Buti na lang may mga bumoses din sa class, kaya na-reconsider at naging over eighty na lang yung score, kasi at that point sobrang drained na kami kaya hindi na rin lahat nakapagsalita sa class. [Baguio/Benguet] It was a sunny afternoon, [REDACTED_NAME] 9th. I had just finished our departmental exam, thrilled to go home and reunite with the warmth and comfort of my bed. I was on my way home, riding a jeep. It was hot and humid inside; the feeling can almost compare to a sardine trapped in a small, tin can. The atmosphere felt heavy. Everyone has their own battles to face, responsibilities to fulfill and goals to achieve. As I looked around, my gaze pinned onto the rearview mirror—giving me the opportunity to observe manong driver. His tired eyes were filled with great sorrow. He let out a mild sigh every once in a while as he maneuvered the steering wheel. He knew every press of the accelerator counted. The wheel spinning was an indication that the engine was consuming amounts of fuel that costs a limb in this currently declining economy. Despite knowing the mechanism of how the jeep operated, manong also knew it was getting harder and harder to survive. With increasing inflation and gas prices—and the economy being at all-time low—a job that does not meet minimum wage yet requires intensive labor obviously feels pointless to endure. It’s heartbreaking to see people suffering while the government acts as if we aren’t facing a major economic crisis. I wish I could do more. I wish I could ease their pain. Next election, vote for a candidate who is capable. Nakaka sawa na, tangina. Maawa naman kayo sa mga pilipino. 🐥 [REDACTED_NAME] 11, 11:05:32 PM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] What’s the point of requiring students to attend face-to-face classes if the entire session is spent passively listening to PowerPoint presentations that could just as easily be delivered online? Physical attendance should be reserved for activities that genuinely benefit from in-person interaction—such as laboratory work, discussions, demonstrations, or collaborative exercises. Otherwise, it becomes an inefficient use of time, energy, and resources for students who often have to commute long distances. Instructors should provide a clear agenda ahead of time, especially for laboratory classes, so students can prepare accordingly and understand whether their presence is truly necessary. A more flexible approach—deciding in advance whether a session will be conducted online or on campus—would allow students to manage their schedules better and avoid unnecessary travel. This is particularly important for those who have multiple classes in a day, where one subject requires them to be physically present, only to have the rest conducted online. The result is wasted time in transit, added financial burden for transportation, and increased fatigue, all of which can negatively impact learning. Additionally, not all students have equal access to reliable transportation. There is also the issue of productivity. Students could use that travel time for studying, completing requirements, or even resting to maintain their well-being. If face-to-face sessions are not being maximized for meaningful engagement, then they risk becoming a formality rather than a valuable educational experience. [REDACTED_NAME] 11, 2:38:08 PM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] this discussion could've been entirely avoided if inanticipate na yung possible events na mangyari rather than maghintay until worse comes to worse? if this was a student, expected na sa OSA palang lagpak na. laging late ah pero far as I've heard, hindi rin valid excuse yung pagiging mabagal sa kanila. lack of action isn't something one can just brush off and accept easily. [Baguio/Benguet] students are held up to a high standard and are expected to become models of competency and innovation. why couldn't the same be said of the school that molds them as such in the first place? let's count: 1 month na since the start of Hormuz blockade. weeks after, projected na yung pag-increase ng oil. government offices already cut down working days soon after. before holy week, anticipated na din na magkakaroon ng transport strike. tapos wala lang? almost little to no consultations nor information about contingency plans such as possible alternative set-ups? look at our contemporaries. medyo masakit lang yung fact na this school is at the forefront when it comes to advocating for societal change yet laging mabagal when it comes to actually making a change. for the drivers, canteens, cafes and bookstores. yeah they are also suffering. however, they are suffering as much as we are suffering. the main source of income is indeed us students. at the same time, inevitable na rin na hihina yung sources of income nila since bababa yung spending power ng students in comparison to pre-energy crisis economy. as saddened as i am about this fact pero we cannot help them completely but soften the blow. we can only help them as far up as much as we can pull ourselves up. case in point? gas drivers are minimizing operations because keeping everything running as before doesn't work na because profits aren't breaking even anymore. as students, sadly this is what we get for normalizing this from the admins. issue na ito last last years pa. hays. we are not "maswerte" just because are demands are meant and our grievances accomodated. we are directly affected in their decisions therefore it is our responsibility to be involved as soon as one potentially comes up. just imagine what could have been done had plans been made and disclosed prior to this week. maybe schedules could be customized by different schools in order to accomodate their demands more (especially yung laboratory and internship-heavy departments). maybe schedules between departments can be alternated so that campus traffic isn't maximized yet services such as canteens and utilities can still be opened. or somehow maybe the one we have ends up being the most efficient for the most people. but doesn't really matter na. too late for that now. [Baguio/Benguet] Ganito kasi yan mga ma'am/sir, kuya/ate or kung sino man makakabasa. Ang kaso kasi sinasabi naman talaga ng mga students kung ano ung sitwasyon na nararanasan nila, kung ano ung problema, at kung ano ang pwede solusyon. Naiintindihan naman namin or ng karamihan na hindi padalos dalos yung pagdedesisyon eh ang kaso nga lang matagal na pong sabi sabi yung 4 days na pasik tas online yung iba. Matagal na yang napropose at yung iba na nga nagpatupad na, in short hindi nman sana aabot sa padalos dalos kung sa simula pa lang napakinggan na yung side ng studyante. Kayo nga pong tumayo sa sitwasyong ng mga studyante, ng ibang mga prof, at ng ibang empleyado na meron sa school namin NA WALANG SASAKYAN, pare parehas lang naman apektado ah pero bakit mas ramdam namin. Idagdag niyo pa yung walang consideration sa OSA, maaga ka na ngang pumila, nagsakripisyo ka ng tulog at trabaho, tas nalate ka pa at mas masaklap kakaratin mo sa school at kalat na nga sa buong bansa nangyayare eh sasabihan ka pa ng "dapat maaga kang pumila". Ngayon may binigay na memo, yun ba ung ibig sabihin ng hinihiling ng mga studyante? Anong hiling ng studyante? Kahit man lang 4 days na pasok tas online... Bakit 4 days? common sense na rin yun eh para balance ba at walang maapakan kasi nga students din lng nakakaalam ng consequences ng kung ano at kami kami din lang makakaramdam... Yung pros and cons na binabash ng mga iba na di naman nag aaral sa aming school, tama naman yun ah. Hindi sa nagrereklamo kami, ang kasi kasi hindi nila yun ginawa (naglabas memo) para sa kapakanan ng studyante...ginawa nila yun para lang di na sila putaktihin ng mga studyante at masalba nila ung imahe na binuo nila. Yun po kasi ung feeling ng iba ngayon... Okay na sana ung 4 days eh, or di kaya ung alternating schedules...kaso binigay ung sagot na para bang di pinag isipan at iisiping tatanggapin nlng basta basta na para bang walang nangyare. At para sa mga studyante din lalo na ung naga yehey yehey sila na buong week walang online tas humirit pa na pati exams at agad agad ipatupad tas magrereklamo din na parang wala din lng kasi papasok para sa lab tas sabi di makakauwi sa probinsya...mga kuya at ate ko, di po ito bakasyon, need lang natin maging flexible para umunlad. yun lang naman at kung sana po sa mga nagbabasa ng freedom wall namin... okay na po na magbasa kayo pakinggan at maki uodate lang kayo pero wala kayong karapatan din po na sabihin na "buti nga kayo...eh nung panahon nga namin" or di kaya "gusto niyo lng punasa ng walang ginagawa" at mas lalo na yung "ang hirap sa inyo gusto niyo masunod yung gusto niyo lang" 😀 Iba na po ngayon sa noon... mas malaking problema na hinaharap namin ngayon na paniguradong pati kayo mahihirapan kung naranasan niyo noon. [REDACTED_NAME] 9, 4:02:35 PM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] Guys, kinalampag niyo ang admin and it worked. Now I know may mga hindi pa rin kayo gusto about the adjustment pero all we can do now is also adjust. Kasi that’s how it works naman diba? You negotiate and when it’s done, you do our part. If you have classmates living near the school then ask them if okay lang mag stay sakanila a day before the exam or class. If nahihiya kang makasagabal sa pag aaral ng kaklase mo, sa library ka mag aral saka ka na lang siguro pumunta sa place niya pag makikitulog ka na. I know hindi yan ang best option due to safety concerns but it’s just a suggestion. I know you can rent this transient rooms pero mahal din yun. Sa mga nakatira naman malapit sa school, I hope you are also open to letting your classmates stay at your place. If you can’t think of other ways to help, maybe consider this one. Also, siguro din ask the admin to extend library hours if necessary or magbukas sila ng rooms that you can use for studying beyond class/office hours. Maximize your tuition. Maybe you can also ask the city to extend the hours sa public library??? Idk baka this can also help students from other schools. If meron din dito nagbabasa na owners ng dorms/apartments, sana po iallow niyo ang tenants to have visitors and have sleep overs at least during the exam period. Please. Mag set na lang siguro ng rules like bawal maingay so others can also review in peace. I know this is all easier said than done and I might even sound nangingialam but I’m just suggesting and I hope it at least gives you an idea how to help one another. Yun lang. I hope we all get through this! I love you all! [REDACTED_NAME] 9, 3:40:30 PM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] Re: [Baguio/Benguet] I understand your point and I do share the sentiment na dapat systematic yung process plus mejo maingay din ang student body recently. Siguro what irked a lot of students isn't that walang nangyayari (syempre we all know logically na meron naman, imposibleng wala), it's that: 1. There seemed to be no consultation of students prior to the long break. And nangyari na ang maraming gastos and issues with OSA. Albeit we have to follow a procedure, it could have been more prompt and expedited. 2. Mejo untimely that this week lang din yung Louisian Pulse survey. And we actually did reach out to admins earlier, I'm not sure of every department pero I know that some did from STELA and SEA. 3. After-the-fact na din kasi nagbigay si [Baguio/Benguet] ng clarification na may conversations na about the matter, kaya nagmukhang washing of hands ang nangyari. I think what [Baguio/Benguet] could do better next time, overall, is to move more quickly. Yes, I understand that it is a very complex problem especially with the size of [Baguio/Benguet]. But I think a lot is at stake din kaya the cost of moving more promptly would be worth it. Also, "dapat kasi una palang inayos nyo na kung ano hinihingi nyo" is a bit problematic. A single solution is bound to NOT fit optimally for everyone, but that is why the Louisian Pulse should have been sent out earlier so that yung diverse na mga hinaing would be better analyzed and options could have been provided for students (if possible). It is [Baguio/Benguet]'s obligation to provide the channel for the grievances, and they arguably have the better capability to sort through complex needs. And for students, it is true, mejo maingay din tayo in not the best way. Kahit na our emotions are boiling, if we keep delivering our grievances this way, we will just be viewed as entitled young people whether our grievances are valid (they are). As for the carwashing issue at ibang baho, that's a conversation for another day. They HAVE to be talked about, but I won't delve on that in this post. [REDACTED_NAME] 9, 12:11:30 PM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] These social issues really made me think about the impact and relevance of our future professions, the contributions and differences that they can make to our economy an society. It's just heartbreaking to see how our PUV drivers and farmers are struggling to make their ends meet. The domino effect also comes in, we also struggle as student commuters, thus our parents must also work harder to provide for us. In short, we DO NOT receive our taxes' worth. As an accountancy student who studies taxation, I can say that this country is F*CK*D-UP. Why? Because our freaking taxes are HIGH, compared to our neighboring countries. Mannn, our VAT (12%) itself is the highest among the Southeast Asian countries. On top of that, we also have other taxes such as the excise, income, withholding, just to name a FEW. These thoughts made me think about the importance of education as our future professions can really make a huge impact to our economy and society. No matter the course/program we are in, we must realize its weight because someday, our professions will make-up our economy. Should we be pressured? At some point, yes, if we're compassionate enough to feel for those who are struggling (drivers, farmers) that we decide to someday make a difference for them, for us. Our education also holds the anchor on our voting knowledge, that by being educated also equates to being wise voters. May the knowledge we have influence us in voting for the right politicians. Hindi para tumulad sa mga bumoto ng dahil lng sa kasikatan at pagkakakilanlan. At since tayong kabataan ang maalam sa tech, research and everything, we must also try our best para i-educate ung mga taong panahon pa ni kopong-kopong ang mindset. Magalit man sila or sabihan tayo ng "anong alam mo jan?" At least we tried our best diba? Gumising-gising ang everyone ha. Di para i-glorify ang mga pulitiko. Isipin nyo na tayo ang boss nila. Bakit? [Baguio/Benguet] Hindi naman sa pangongontra ng protesta kanina para magpakita ng solidarity para sa mga tsuper at operator ng jeep, pero hindi ko mapigilang magtaka tungkol sa mga plaka at tarp na hinahawakan ng mga nagpoprotesta kanina. I know the protesters are smart enough to include foresight in their stances kasi most of them are either exposed to the societal environment that these drivers are facing or pinag aaralan nila mismo kasi kasama sa kurso nila, pero stances like "No to oil price hike" and "Tanggalin ang VAT and Excise Tax sa Langis, Pagkain, at Utilities" are completely contradictory to one another because how will you stop the oil price hike (hindi ibig sabihin na maglalagay ka ng price ceiling eh oil companies will just follow along willingly, the government has to subsidize their expense kasi if they don't, edi titigil nalang sa operasyon yung kompanya) and also stop a massive source of income for the government to fund the subsidized price ceiling. Dapat lang suspendihin yung VAT, Excise Tax, and Train Law sa langis, pero on the other things, disadvantage na ang publiko dun. Isang bagay sana na mas makakabenepisyo sa mga jeep na hindi nila pinaskil is targeted automatic fuel subsidies sa mga PUV mismo para yung pera ng gobyerno ay dumiretso sa mga nangangailangan. Kung tutuusin, mas benepisyo pa nga yung mayayaman yung pagtanggal ng VAT at Excise Tax kesa sa mga jeep kasi sila yung nakakapag full tank ng sasakyan nila kumpara sa jeep na litro litro lang yung karga. Another point they made is to nationalize the oil industry in the country, like norway and saudi arabia, yet they don't take into account na sobrang mismanaged at corrupt na nga yung ibang government owned corporations natin tulad ng PAGCOR at PhilHealth, paano pa kaya yung corporation na kumokontrol sa langis, isa sa pinaka importante at ma-perang industriya sa buong bansa. Hindi siya magiging solusyon kundi isa nanaman siyang paraan para manakawan tayo. That's another reason rin kung bakit risky na i-junk yung oil deregulation law, kasi it might open up an essential industry to the crocodiles from the philippine government (mind you, wala rin naman kasiguraduhan na if they control the prices, kaya nila ibaba sa presyong abot kaya especially with the ongoing international situation right now that they have no control over). Paano mo rin naman papanagutin ng maayos yung mga magnanakaw kung bibigyan mo ulit sila ng pandagdag na industriyang pwedeng babuyin. Nagpakita at nakisimpatya yung mga nag protesta upang suportahan ang mga tsuper at komyuter na nahihirapan sa araw-araw at hindi mawawala sakanila ang pasasalamat at suporta ng karamihan ng mga luwisyano, ngunit katumbas o mas importante yung mga mensaheng inilalabas at ipinapaskil natin sa mga taong nakikinig. Kung ang mensahe na yun ay nagkulang sa prudensiya at pilit na binabahiran ng kulay ng isang politikal na paksyon at nakikisabay sa pagpapasikat ng ibang mga personalidad, hindi na siya nagiging dalisay na suporta para sa tunay na nangangailangan. [REDACTED_NAME] 8, 11:18:47 PM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] Great evening, everyone. Okay, so before you judge me agad sa mga sasabihin ko... I AM thankful sa new memo, hyflex learning modality. I'm not an ungrateful b*tch naman. I know we requested for consideration regarding our current situation. Some, if not most, requested for a four-day F2F and two-day online classes. That's enough na for some of us. I know naman na the new learning modality is more beneficial to our daily lives in terms of financial aspect and such. No more pila. No more wasted time na naghihintay ng jeep na hindi dadating even after an hour of waiting. Save energy, save money, and the likes. But do you think na it will benefit us katulad sa F2F classes? No, right? Mawawala na yung momentum ng students. Not only the students pero pati yung mga professors. In some classes, students can't even understand that fast yung mga lessons na tinuturo ng prof, especially if yung prof is hindi ganon kagaling in terms of teaching. I'm not saying na bb sila they're qualified, I know that. Magaling sila, pero bihira yung mga teaching employees na very effective and efficient. Yung tipong skip skip lang pero sa sobrang galing nya magturo is gets mo agad. Some profs don't even discuss, they just give you YouTube links, a bunch of it. Learning modules and such, then you study on your own then magpapaactivity. I know, I know, as student we also have our responsibilities, dapat mag aral rin tayo. If you're a slow learner then be more masipag. If you can't understand a lesson then study it until you can explain like it's just a basic concept. This hyflex learning modality will surely help us a lot. We all know that. Maraming students na hindi ganon kabilis matuto sa online classes, plus, marly mga prof rin na tamad magturo sa online classes. As I've said kanina magsesend lang sila ng YouTube videos then hindi na nila ididiscuss. We requested. You responded. Pero in my case, maybe it's not only me who thinks like this, maraming disadvantage yung gantong modality. Of course I want to say more but I think y'all know what my point is. Some, if not most, will think that this F2F, ONLINE, F2F, ONLINE is not the best solution for this crisis honestly. Still, thank you. [REDACTED_NAME] 7, 6:20:58 PM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] Hi, I just wanna vent about the professor who's making Capstone ABSOLUTE hell for some students. :) To begin, when my team was currently getting adjusted to the Capstone project already having plans and a Gantt chart all set up, the professor (will be referred to as Mr.1) starts the semester off by scheduling a face-to-face meeting @ 4:30PM to 5:30PM to talk about what he wants for the project proper. Nothing else. Why did he want face-to-face instead of synchronous, I don't know. Afterwards, he begins to talk about how his previous blocks never failed when he was the advisor, and that he's expecting us to continue that streak, and he proceeds to talk about that for HALF of the the time, and THEN begins talking about the requirements when it's about 10-20 minutes before the time. Not only that, he took half of that time just talking about Chapter 1. The thing is that after that class, we had to go home during a heavy storm @ 6:07PM. If anything, we could've ended at 5:30PM if it weren't for him just telling us about his students' success. Now, next thing I want to complain about: During one of our scheduled meetings for "consultation". I'm using HEAVY QUOTATION MARKS for consultation. When it was time to meet and some of our groupmates couldn't make it punctually, we asked Mr. 1 to start the meeting and inform the late members of what we talked about, but then he INSISTED that it's either all eight of us were attending, or there WILL BE NO MEETING. Excuse me, but just because someone in the group is missing does NOT mean that he can just not do the meeting entirely. That's not fair to even those who attended early. Not only that, when the actual meeting DID commence, one of the members was constantly disconnecting due to an internet problem. He tried to explain that he couldn't keep a stable connection but Mr. 1 said that he was "making up excuses" and if he doesn't attend the meeting, he's gonna get kicked out. I spoke with the member at the time, and he did confirm that his WiFi was cutting out cause he was in town with his family and couldn't keep a stable connection. If anything, that's not fair to the student if he gets kicked out for having bad WiFi. He then proceed to ask about how the leadership structure works. For context: we agreed that we get to work on our pace and still be able to deliver a quality output when the deadline comes, since we know that we have other academic courses to tend to and this was the most viable option. Mr. 1 then got mad at that structure, and demanded that our group leader was more stricter with deadlines, and demanded we do daily reports, which is just absolutely unnecessary. WHY? The team already HAS A RHYTHM that we can get going and he says that it's not viable? I thought teamwork meant that we get to find the most optimal way for everyone to work together, not turn the group into a corporation simulator. AND TO ADD!! Yes, this is an entire disaster in it of itself: when we showed him our paper, Mr. 1 complained it was too simple, and I quote: "If I put this into AI, I bet it will be longer." I'M SORRY, BUT WHY WOULD YOU IMPLY THAT WE USE AI? It's a CAPSTONE PROJECT. I dunno if it's because he has some sort of bias towards a certain course, but that's just disrespectful to the team that was working on the paper. I've heard that in another group, he was INSISTING that they use AI in their research paper!! You wouldn't believe the cherry on top of this cake, but when my group's leader asked for any tips or advice on improvement on the paper, Mr. 1 REFUSED to give any! Citing his reason, "did you not listen to my discussion during our face-to-face orientation?" We asked what he meant, and he refused to reply at all or even elaborate on what he meant. We listened to his lessons, but all we remember is the LITTLE INFORMATION HE GAVE ABOUT THE REQUIREMENTS AND MORE ABOUT HIM BOASTING HIS PREVIOUS CLASS. I'm sorry, but I cannot work with a professor like that. What kind of professor not only DOWNPLAYS his class's work, but then proceeds to GLAZE AI USAGE for an ACADEMIC PROJECT that's supposed to PUT US STUDENTS TO THE TEST. When the project was proposed, he was suggesting making something more coding focused than an actual product. I'm not mad per se, I'm more confused on HOW he had the audacity to say such. Thank you po for listening to my vent! :) [REDACTED_NAME] 7, 12:36:28 PM HKT

ADVISORY | The [Baguio/Benguet] switches to a new Alternating HYFLEX Weekly Modality In response to the ongoing national energy crisis, [Baguio/Benguet] has implemented an alternating weekly learning modality to ensure continuity of education while considering transportation challenges and student welfare. After the Midterms examinations, learning modality will shift between face-to-face and online on a weekly basis, with major examinations and essential academic activities remaining on-site. Clinical duties, internships, and other hands-on requirements will continue face-to-face throughout the semester as part of non-negotiable academic engagements. Student services, including the health clinic and the school library, will remain available. Should you encounter any issues with institutional accounts, you may contact the university ISTSD email for assistance in resolving them. You may also reach out to the KASAMA/SSC for any issues that would arise.

[Baguio/Benguet] I’m a 3rd year med student, and this is one of those random moments that just stayed with me. It was a rainy afternoon, can’t vividly remember when this happened but it was around first semester. I was by the main gate, waiting for the rain to ease a bit. Then I noticed this student nurse. Kakagaling lang siguro sa duty. She was in a pink uniform with a pink jacket, then she opened her umbrella, about to head out. Beside her was another girl, a complete stranger, walang dalang umbrella. They talked for a bit, then she simply shared her umbrella with her. No hesitation. Sabay silang tumawid like it was the most normal thing in the world. Her building was just across the gate, and I saw her go in after making sure the other girl was okay. Hindi ko man nakita mukha niya clearly, but that small act stuck with me. In that moment, I had a little more faith in humanity. And maybe, just a small crush moment too. Wherever you are, I hope you keep being that kind of person. You’re meant for this field. Maybe we’ll cross paths someday, this time inside the hospital. 🙂 [REDACTED_NAME] 4, 6:40:22 PM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] This is my very first entry. I've been keeping this to myself pero ngayon hindi ko na talaga kaya. To the BSCHE department, something might be going on with you. I'm an irregular student. I kept telling myself it's my fault, kinulang ako. Naging paubaya sa sarili ko. Maybe.. Pwede nga naman na medyo pinabayaan ko ang sarili ko. Pero may napansin ako. I took Enggmath # from the other department with some of my friends. Our grades? Hindi bababa sa 95. Pero yung friends namin na kumuha ng same Enggmath # sa department niyo? Pasang-awa 75 na lang. A friend of ours took the Enggchem lab subject sa kabilang department. Problema lang nila? Data. Pero kaming CHE? Handwritten dapat. Minimal dapat ang erasure, and everything. More than half of our batch in this department became irregular. I kept trying to switch my perspective na kami ang may kasalanan. Na may pagkukulang kami. Na kulang kami sa review and everything. But everytime I ask my friends na nag-shift sa ibang department kung kamusta sila, sobrang layo daw ang hirap sa department natin. At kung tutuusin, napakahirap niyo naman talaga eh. [Baguio/Benguet] kept telling us to rest, to relax, kahit this holy week lang, pero this department? Ang dami daming homework. It's not our fault na madaming suspensions or holiday na nakatapat sa sched natin on this particular subject. Na kailangan ninyong gawing homework lahat. Handwritten lab reports? Fine! Hand-drawn set-up sa lab reports? Sanay na kami! Too much items to answer in a 1 hour quiz? Sige po adjust kami! Pero late profs everytime na may quiz? Profs na maliit ang sulat pero nagbibigay ng deductions sa student na maliit ang sulat? Too much homework this holy week?! Okay pa po ba kayo? Kaya gets ko na kung bakit minimal ang space ng CHE— wala ng CHE grounds, walang sariling building, and may rumor from the seniors na kukunin na ang CHE LAB—kasi parang hindi na worth it ipaglaban ang BSCHE sa [Baguio/Benguet]! [Baguio/Benguet] “Is it right to feel lost? To feel like the path I am taking now doesn’t seem to be my true calling?” These are the thoughts that have been lingering inside my mind for the past few months na. What’s funny is graduating student na ako. In fact, I am already an intern. I am grieving the way I used to be back in first year kung saan nandoon yung fire, yung “rage” ba na kung ano na-envision ko sa sarili ko noong senior high ako or even high school, eh matutupad ko na kasi after college, law school na lang gugugulin ko. Mabilis lang naman lumipas ang panahon kahit hindi yung hardship na pagdadaanan ko but at least, I know I will get there. Pero now, grabe ang burnout ko. Hindi ko alam kung itutuloy ko pa pumasok sa law. Gusto ko ng puso ko, pero yung katawan at utak ko yung umaayaw. Nappressure ako sa tuwing nakikita ko mga ka-batch kong busy mag-prepare for law admission exams, nagtatanungan kung saang law school nag-enroll. Gusto ko sana. Pagod lang ako masyado. Valid ba ‘tong nararamdaman ko? Sabi kasi nila kapag gusto, may paraan. Kung ayaw, may dahilan. Pero sobrang unprepared ako mentally and physically. Emotionally, oo. But it takes all of them to tango sa law school yata eh. My parents are willing to support my law school journey naman. It’s just me who’s the problem ngayon kasi feel ko hindi ko naman calling maging abogado o maging political scientist (PolSci student, btw) pero wala eh, nagugol ko na yung apat na taon. Pa-graduate na ako. Ayun lang. Siguro for now, the least I could do is to give myself time and reflect kung ano ba talaga gusto ko sa buhay. Sobrang naliligaw lang as of the moment. Thank you. To my PolSci batchmates, padayon! I’m very well aware na naman na hindi naman ako nag-iisa sa mga gantong klaseng feeling, so goodluck to our own journeys after we graduate! :) March 29, 3:33:29 PM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] I watched [Baguio/Benguet] CCAS’ Hunchback of Notre Dame on March 24, and it was absolutely amazing. From start to finish, the entire cast delivered. All the leads were genuinely good. each one brought something strong and memorable to the stage. Ramdam mo talaga yung effort, emotion, and passion in every scene. But I have to say, Frollo really stood out the most for me. Grabe yung intensity niya, every line hit hard. And his diction? Sobrang linaw. Parang may subtitles every time he spoke you catch every word, every emotion, every thought. Nakaka-amaze. During Hellfire, you could literally feel the entire audience on the edge of their seats. The atmosphere he created was so tense, so powerful, that it pulled everyone in completely. Walang nakahinga nang maayos, lahat tutok lang sa kanya. That kind of presence is rare, and he owned that moment. And the orchestra? They delivered extremely well. The music didn’t just support the show...it elevated everything. Every note felt intentional, adding depth and emotion that made the entire experience even more powerful. More than anything, this production truly inspired me. There’s something about it that ignited a fire in me a push to finally try acting as well. Overall, super solid production. Definitely one of those shows na tatatak talaga. March 28, 8:30:23 PM HKT
