[Baguio/Benguet] To CCA and everyone involved in [Baguio/Benguet]’s production of Hunchback of Notre Dame: Thank you for making my first theatre experience as magical as I expected it to be. Hunchback of Notre Dame Day 3 watcher here! Happy vent ko lang to sa fw cause wala akong ibang friends na mahilig sa theatre Na discover ko theatre ngayong college and nakakanood lang ako through “slime tutorials” or mga bootlegs na makikita or mattrade mo kung saan saan. Di ako makanood in person kasi wala namang kahit anong major theatre musicals / plays na pinoproduce around Baguio and masyadong expensive naman ang pag biyahe sa Manila para manood ng isang show. So imagine my surprise nung naglabas yung CCA ng sarili nilang production ng Hunchback of Notre Dame! Was so excited to see it. Fast forward sa mismong day of the performance and to be honest i kept my expectations low. First time ko manood ng theatre in person and di ko din alam kung ano dapat i expect ko HAHA. Pero the moment na nag simula na tumugtog yung orchestra at nakita kong kinakanta na yung “Bells of Notre Dame”, alam ko na agad na this is something special. Insanely solid production, and ang magical ng experience pag nandon ka talaga sa teatro😭 Yung technical aspects din pala like set design, lighting and orchestra was downright phenomenal. Apat na taon na ako sa [Baguio/Benguet], and super thankful akong naranasan ko to bago ako umalis sa last sem ko. Kudos sa lahat ng taong involved dito, nabuhay nyo ang Notre Dame para maexperience naming manonood. Noteworthy performances for me would be kay Quasimodo, Esmeralda at Frollo. Favorite ko pala yung Frollo performance kasi nakuha mo yung aura na pinakita ni [REDACTED_NAME] Page sa original cast. Hellfire performance with the booming orchestra Tapos na ata ako mag ramble. Hopefully many more productions like this to come in the future. Kahit maging alumni na ako, babalik at babalik ako dahil sa quality na pinakita ng performance na to sa akin. Salamat sa pagbuhay ng teatro sa ating unibersidad! March 28, 3:11:32 PM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] [[LONG TEXT INCOMING]] some bits of wisdom from a 4th year psych na desidido na since high school (na naaapply sa psych pero baka pwede rin sa ibang programs): 1. papalit-palit ang ideal career mo sa college, and it's okay. when i was in hs, i told myself, "magiging therapist talaga ako kasi i want to fix what's wrong with me" (not a feasible reason btw ngl) but throughout my 4 years, i found myself liking i/o more (shoutout to maam tin wahhh miss you maam) nung third year na ako. point is you get to explore more options during your college life, it's up to you to find what works for you. 2. if sure ka na sa psych, pLEASE LOCK IN SA MGA MAJOR COURSES NIYO. ituturo rin ulit mga necessary sa course enrichment pero if you lock in early, magiging breeze na lang sa inyo yung next years kasi more or less makikita niyo rin yung same lesson every year. 3. find balance between work and fun. let loose. have some whimsy in your life. (pero be responsible, ha? wag yung walwalan nang walwalan or whatev.) kept me sane enough to survive. 4. you gotta love research, unfortunately. siyempre madami eh. sa dami ba naman ng articles na babasahin mo akalain mo masters ka na. pero it's lowkey fun to read what you need and how it connects. isa ang psych program ng [Baguio/Benguet] sa mga top-notch sa country. bilib ka, top performing ang [Baguio/Benguet] sa blepp for consecutive years. kakayanin natin yan! have fun! 🍧 March 26, 9:12:23 PM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] Hello, senior & other readers. I’ve always lurked on here, but today I decided to send one in as a reply to the post tagged on this post. I’ve learned of a beautiful quote when I was feeling behind when my peers graduated earlier than me. The quote said that the only competition I have is that of myself. If life was a race, I’d be behind future me, and yesterday’s me would be behind me. That should be the only competition you have. Not you vs. other people. Everyone else has their own race to run. So stay in your lane and focus on surpassing the future version of you. With that being said, I hope you’re doing well now, you are more than the accolades you have. You are so much more. You are a person who has hobbies, and interests. Those things matter too, not just your accomplishments. Focus on what enriches your life. Not just on what other people expects from you. Please please please learn to live for yourself, and please believe that the things you want now, will all be yours in due time. With the right amount of effort, courage, and love from the ones who matter the most (not just romantically), you’ll be just fine. If no one has said it yet, I’m very proud of you for making progress. Take healing one step at a time, and if you revert back to any old habits, please don’t beat yourself up over it. Instead, be the kindest to yourself when you slip up. This is your reminder that you should never abandon yourself if you feel like the world has abandoned you. No matter how harsh the world is right now, never be harsh towards yourself. You don’t deserve that. Stay safe, and don’t forget to be kind to yourself. Sending hugs with consent to everyone, 🐈‍⬛ March 26, 8:51:15 PM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] I’ll just rant here for a bit. I admit I got interested in this guy last year when we became classmates. At first, I thought it was nothing more than curiosity about his personality. But slowly, it turned into something I couldn’t quite handle—seeing him often, being around him, and feeling something I kept trying to deny. Honestly, I was frustrated with myself. I kept insisting it was just curiosity—not a crush, not anything deeper. But I couldn’t ignore the quiet disappointment when I realized he wasn’t the person I imagined him to be. You know that feeling when the version of someone in your mind doesn’t match who they really are? Yeah... that. As someone who values boundaries—especially with other girls—I kept everything lowkey. Even my friend here at [Baguio/Benguet] didn’t notice a thing; she was genuinely surprised when she found out. I guess that’s my “talent”... knowing how to hide what I feel. But my closest friends back in my province know the truth—I usually keep my distance from guys. They’ve always been a bit of a pet peeve of mine (sorry 😭). Right now, all I want is to finish this semester and quietly walk away—from them, and especially from him. This is the first time I’ve ever felt like I was losing control over my own emotions. Before, I never allowed myself to go this deep into someone’s world... not until I met him. Still, I’m thankful. Somehow, he gave me reasons to step back and see things clearly. He’s caring and reliable, I’ll give him that—but he’s also a people pleaser. Even when he doesn’t genuinely like someone, he still stays, still talks, still blends in. He’s friends with everyone, and maybe that’s not a flaw. Maybe I just expected him to be different—to be something more. Now, I’ve started avoiding him, and I think he’s noticed. But it doesn’t really matter anymore. Maybe this is how it was always supposed to be—distance and clarity. I’ve never had a boy best friend, and I’ve always said I didn’t like guys... which makes this whole thing feel a little ironic. I can’t believe I almost lost myself in something that was never really mine. Thankfully, I was able to pull away. Two more months of passing encounters, and then I’m free. In the end, it was never me vs. him—it was always me vs. me 😂 March 26, 5:04:10 PM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] This is my first time writing in a freedom wall of [Baguio/Benguet]. Sa tagal ko dito, I think it's about time to share something I've been bottling up. Hi. I'm one of your senior (219) schoolmate. Hindi naman ako bobo, hindi rin tamad and I think I'm friendly naman. So why am I still studying if I'm basically an adult now. Walang anak at wala ring work. I've been thinking and overthinking that I'm too late for life, for family, for a career. And yet I can't seem to move forward. It's been years since I was bullied inside the premises of this school. Graduate na nga yung mga yun pero why can't I move on. Habang sila may license na or probably may family na. And here I am. Questioning my existence over and over again. Yung feeling na parang everyone judge you kahit wala naman silang paki sa totoo lang. Words really cut deep. I hope anyone who reads this, sana pagisipan niyo muna kung yung lalabas sa bunganga niyo before spilling it out. A simple word of judgement from you may be the biggest trauma for other people. I just want to say always choose to be kind. And so far these younger students after our batch are much nicer and more gentle. And by the way. Graduating na ako 😁. I appreciate the little smiles and approachable vibes of the students na nakakasalubong ko sa hallways. You guys are helping me heal what's broken. I was diagnosed pala with PTSD, Anxiety disorder and severe depression. And guess what, even though I'm not fully okay. At least I'm making progress little by little. And the new environment of the hallways that once haunted me of judgement and trauma changed because of the new people in it. Don't forget to check on your friends. Mental health is not "lang". It is way serious than you think. But with the right people that surrounds and understands you. You're in a safe space. March 26, 11:21:51 AM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] Sorry if not [Baguio/Benguet] related pero genuine question, wala po bang magagawa ang school sa Bullying Cases? Nag ooverthink kasi ako at the moment, and to think of it dadalawa lang kami ng kapatid ko sa Dorm and ako yung tumatayong guardian niya tho he's Junior High. Naka ilang absences na ako dahil sa meeting and ayoko din paakyatin yung parents ko dahil sa mahal ng pamasahe tho aware sila sa nangyayari sa amin. Recently umakyat na yung case ng kapatid ko sa DepEd Division Office and hindi ko na natake yung sinabi ng principal ng school. Why? I heard her said that "Kaya ayokong humahawak ng ganitong mga kaso eh." while I hand her the papers ng mga evidences ng pambubully sa kapatid ko. Natulala ako kasi parang walang intervention ang school regarding bullying. Ang mas nakakasakit pa sa akin bilang nakakatandang kapatid, kinausap ako ng isa sa mga head ng DepEd. The conversation goes like this: ; Sir kung may nangyare sa kapatid ko, may pananagutan po ba yung school? : Pwede naman po magkaso. ; Sir iisa lang po buhay ng kapatid ko, paano po? Aantayin nyo po ba na may mangyare bago po tayo aaksyon? : Eh ganun po talaga, wala po tayong magagawa eh. Nasaktan ako sa totoo lang kasi parang kahit yung Division office ng Baguio Walang magawa sa ganitong case. Also, kailangan ko tong ivent out kasi di ko alam sasabihin ko sa OSA kung nagkataon. March 26, 8:07:17 AM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] Continuation of [Baguio/Benguet] They only ever spoke through a screen. It started as nothing—just a shared document passed around between students from UC and [Baguio/Benguet] for a joint project no one really cared about. Names were listed, roles assigned, deadlines ignored. Navi didn’t plan to notice one name in particular. Benjie. No profile picture. No introductions. Just edits—clean, precise, almost annoyingly perfect. At first, Navi found it irritating. Every time he left a messy draft, it would be fixed within the hour. Grammar corrected. Ideas tightened. Even his phrasing—changed just enough to make it better. So he did what anyone would do. He started leaving mistakes on purpose. And Benjie kept fixing them. Days passed like that—silent back-and-forths hidden in tracked changes. Navi would write something slightly off, and Benjie would adjust it. No comments. No notes. Just quiet understanding. Until one night, Navi left a line untouched. Not part of the project. Just... there. “Do you ever get tired of fixing things that aren’t yours?” He didn’t expect a response. But the next morning, the document had changed. The line was still there. And beneath it— “Only when they keep breaking on purpose.” Navi stared at the screen longer than he should’ve. Then he smiled. So that’s how it was. From then on, the document wasn’t just a project anymore. It became a conversation. Not obvious—never direct—but threaded between paragraphs and bullet points. “You’re from UC?” Navi typed once, hidden between citations. A few minutes later— “Does it matter?” “A little.” “...[Baguio/Benguet]?” “Yeah.” There was a pause. Longer than usual. Then— “Figures.” Navi laughed quietly to himself. They never exchanged socials. Never sent messages outside that document. But somehow, it felt more honest that way. No faces. No voices. Just words—carefully chosen, deliberately placed. Until the night before the deadline. Navi opened the file, expecting the usual. But something was off. The document was... finished. Perfect. Clean. No mistakes left for him to make. No edits left for Benjie to fix. And at the very bottom— A final line. “This is enough.” Navi frowned. Enough? That wasn’t how this worked. He typed quickly, fingers moving before he could think. “That’s it?” The cursor blinked. Once. Twice. Then— “Yes.” Something in his chest tightened. “We’re not even done talking.” A longer pause this time. So long Navi thought maybe— “We were never supposed to start.” His jaw clenched. “That didn’t stop you.” No response. Navi leaned closer to the screen, like it would make the silence easier to break. “What, you’re just going to disappear after this?” Minutes passed. Then— “It’s better that way.” Navi exhaled sharply, running a hand through his hair. “For who?” No answer. Of course. He stared at the words, at the neat, polished document that suddenly felt... empty. Like something had been carefully erased. “You always do this?” he typed. This time, the reply came slower. “Do what?” “Leave before things mean something.” The cursor blinked again. Longer. Heavier. Then— “You don’t even know me.” Navi’s fingers hovered over the keyboard. Then pressed down. “I know enough.” Another pause. And for a second—just a second—Navi thought maybe Benjie would argue. Deny it. Stay. But instead— “Goodbye, Navi.” The name hit harder than it should’ve. Because Navi never told him. Not directly. Not anywhere obvious. Yet somehow— He knew. Navi’s throat felt dry. “Wait—” But the cursor stopped blinking. The document went still. No more edits. No more replies. Just a finished project... and a conversation that ended too cleanly to feel real. Navi stared at the screen until it dimmed. “...You’re seriously just going to leave it like that?” he muttered. But there was no one left to answer. Only that last word sitting quietly at the bottom— Goodbye. And somehow, that felt like the only thing Benjie ever fixed that Navi couldn’t undo. Ps. [Baguio/Benguet] Tama siya, matagal pa ang student council elections, but with everything that is happening in the council lately, calls for a better council. I've tried researching and curating a list of active Louisians who, I think, should run and would do a great job inside the council. So, this is my [Baguio/Benguet] SSC Elections presidential race wishlist: Starting of with the obvious, 1. Jesamine Ramolete - Current STELA Governor - Tama yung sinabi ni sender, talked about ang stela assembly, sobrang active, parang weekly ata ako nakakakita ng activity nila, kung hindi naman, mga sensing forms at statements. It feels like parang lahat ng nasa platforms nila nung elections ay nagawa na. - Also a very vocal activist, active and a critical thinker based sa mga past interviews niya, which i think kailangan nating quality ng isang president, maalam at kilala ang lipunan. - Also, all that just under her governance? crazy. - Comparing also to all the current governors, she feels like the rebel and persisent one in the group (in a good way). - It also feels like siya na ang grinu-groom ng dasig ngayon for the highest position, kasi kung hindi siya, sino pa ba? 2. Kathlyn Tudlong - Current [DEPARTMENT] Governor - Isang executive committee material type din. Pareho ng sa STELA, [DEPARTMENT] feels so active with their projects. - Basing on what I see, close ang assembly nila, lahat sila friends, and it's good that they can maintain it that way para may better working environment sila. - She knows how to listen, parang more of the doer siya than the thinker for me, possible for VP pero onting piga ay kaya niya rin magpresident. - Friendly and understanding, bukod sa kailangan ito ng students, kailangan itong quality niya for admin dialogues. Although ingat lang para hindi magmukhang pro-admin. - She honestly can ran with Jome last elections sa ExeCom, if not better than him. Although, again, mas naiintindihan kong mas kailangan siya ng [DEPARTMENT] at mas mauutilize siya ron. 3. [REDACTED_NAME] Lagamia - Activist - Nakikita ko lang siya sa mga rallies, laging nagsasalita. Knew his name dahil sa walkout din. - Sobrang galing na activist in my opinion, I read some of his interviews from different pubs, everything felt so sharp, lalo na yung interview niya sa TOFI, it almost seemed like siya yung president dahil ganon dapat ang sagot ng isa. - Just like Jesamine, he also feels like a rebel (in a good way again). Mukhang kaya niyang makipagsagutan sa admin and we need that in a council, at least magkaroon strong one. - I don't know if I'll consider him a dark horse pa, pero chairperson na kasi siya ng WILN, DASIG, Bahaghari, or at least naging, based only in articles. - I also think it's time for an activist to lead the council, feeling ko mas naiintindihan kasi nila tayong students. 4. [REDACTED_NAME] Del Castillo - Former Executive Secretary - Watched her MDA performance, she looks powerful, it actually felt like siya yung president sa repo slate last year. - Been in the council since freshmen ata? so probably alam niya na ang pasikot-sikot nito which is something important. - Looking at it, siya ata laging representative ng SSC in rallies. - And maybe just like [REDACTED_NAME], basing sa interviews niya, sharp speaker din. - Feels approachable and sure of what she's saying. In my opinion, we need leaders stronger than this, pero valid pa rin kasi active leader naman siya. - She also feels like she can converse well with the admin, baka negative pakinggan pero mukhang maibabalance niya naman since activist din siya. Anyway, those are just popular names I usually hear, if not seen in articles. [Baguio/Benguet] Navi always noticed him first in crowded places. Not because Benjie stood out—he didn’t. He blended too well, like someone who learned early that being seen too much meant being asked too many questions. But Navi had a way of catching the small things: the way Benjie lingered near exits, the way his shoulders tensed whenever someone called out “UC,” the way he never stayed long enough for conversations to settle into something real. They weren’t supposed to meet like this. [Baguio/Benguet] and UC didn’t mix—not in the ways that mattered. Not in the quiet, late-night study halls where exhaustion softened people into honesty. Not in the spaces where rivalry dissolved and something more dangerous could take its place. Yet there Benjie was, sitting across from him like it wasn’t wrong. “You’re staring again,” Benjie muttered without looking up. Navi huffed, resting his chin on his hand. “You came here. I think I’m allowed to stare.” “I didn’t come for you.” “Liar.” Benjie’s pen paused. For a second—just a second—Navi thought he’d look up. Thought their eyes would meet and everything unsaid would spill between them like it always almost did. But Benjie just kept writing. “You shouldn’t be here,” he said quietly. Navi leaned back, stretching like he didn’t care. Like it didn’t sting. “And you should?” Silence. The kind that pressed. The kind that said more than either of them wanted. Outside, someone laughed—too loud, too bright. It reminded Navi of everything they weren’t. “I heard,” Navi started, voice lighter than he felt, “you’re transferring.” That did it. Benjie looked up. Finally. And God, Navi hated that his chest tightened at something so simple. “Who told you that?” “So it’s true.” Benjie exhaled, sharp. “It doesn’t matter.” “It does to me.” The words slipped out before he could stop them. Before he could make them sound like a joke. Benjie stared at him like he’d just crossed a line neither of them had named. “That’s exactly why it shouldn’t,” Benjie said, quieter now. “You know how this ends.” Navi laughed, but it cracked. “No, I don’t. Why don’t you tell me?” “You go back to [Baguio/Benguet]. I go back to UC. And whatever this is—” he gestured vaguely between them, like it disgusted him to define it, “—stays here.” “Like it never happened?” “Yes.” The word came too fast. Too rehearsed. Navi swallowed. “You’re good at that.” “At what?” “Pretending things don’t matter.” Benjie’s jaw tightened. “Not everything has to.” “That’s not true.” “Then what do you want, Navi?” His voice rose just enough to draw a glance from someone nearby before dropping again. “You want me to fight for this? For something that’s already—” “Don’t,” Navi cut in. Benjie stopped. “Don’t say it like it’s already over,” Navi whispered. Because if he did, then it would be real. And Navi wasn’t ready for real. Not like that. For a moment, Benjie looked like he might reach across the table. His hand twitched—barely noticeable unless you were looking for it. Navi was. But he didn’t. Of course he didn’t. Benjie pulled his hand back, curling it into a fist instead. “It’s easier this way.” “For who?” No answer. Navi let out a breath, shaking his head. “You know what I think?” Benjie didn’t respond, but he didn’t look away either. “I think you’re scared.” A flicker. There, and gone. “I think,” Navi continued, softer now, “you’d rather lose something before it has the chance to matter too much.” “That’s not—” “It is.” Navi smiled, but it hurt. “Because if it matters, then leaving actually hurts, right?” Benjie stood abruptly, chair scraping against the floor. “We’re done here.” Navi didn’t move. Didn’t stop him. Because this was how it always went—almosts and almosts and almosts until there was nothing left to hold onto. Benjie hesitated. Just one step away. “Don’t come here again,” he said, not turning back. Navi watched him go. Watched the space he left behind like it might fill itself if he stared long enough. It didn’t. “...You came first,” Navi murmured to the empty chair. And somehow, that made it worse. Because if Benjie hadn’t come— Then Navi wouldn’t have had anything to lose in the first place. Ps: Na inspire lang Kay ate [REDACTED_NAME] nung Saturday hehe (peace✌️) March 23, 12:11:37 PM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] Student Council elections are still a bit far off, but with everything that’s been happening in the council lately, it feels like a good time to reflect on how the different political parties have been performing. This is just personal overall assessment based on observations and experiences, trying to stay as objective as possible. REFORMA-ACS, center-left (left?) - has been around in the council for a long time and clearly has experience and institutional memory. - personal fave: Gab [REDACTED_NAME]’s term (grounded and more connected to student realities) - They’ve also been involved in negotiations on student concerns (capable of engaging in policy discussions when needed) - recurring concerns that tend to follow the party (instances where their presence in the council felt inconsistent or inactive, especially outside of peak political moments) - During elections, they are often associated with controversies (internal conflicts to allegations of aggressive tactics) which raises questions about their political culture (problematic daw sabi ng mga from repo na friends) - endorsements and alliances, can also come off as unclear or questionable to some students, making it harder to understand what they firmly stand for. - criticisms about the kinds of candidates they put forward, with some being seen as not fully prepared for the responsibilities of governance. - campaigns are usually polished, strategic, there’s a noticeable gap at times between what is promised and what is actually delivered once in office, which has led to skepticism among some students. 5/10 - hoping na mas consistent sana when it comes to what is promised and what is implemented. DASIG, left perspective, (wala ako masabi aside from puro disruptive protest-good way) - is very visible when it comes to student issues and activism. - show up consistently (pwera nung 1st years ko? ewan nag peak lang sila nung kay heaven ulit and this yr) pero present parin sila sa rally sa labas ng paaralan, in campaigns and in movements related to tuition concerns, dress codes, and even broader national issues. - more militant and advocacy-driven, which works well for students who want a louder and more assertive representation (tho polarizing, especially for those who prefer quieter or more negotiation-based approaches.) - strong presence and engagement with student concerns are hard to ignore. - Their performance can also be seen in the current STELA Assembly, where they appear to be very grounded in terms of representing student sentiments. - evident ang stela assembly. they are frequently talk about them and their initiatives, which suggests that their visibility and connection with students extend across year levels. 100/10 RIGHTScenter-left - seems to take a more balanced and diplomatic approach. - generally good working relationship with the administration, which helps in negotiating and pushing for solutions in a more formal setting - [DEPARTMENT] assembly (wish na ma expand din sila sa main campus with dasig{sana mag collab}) members also tend to have solid leadership backgrounds, which adds to their credibility. -they may not always be as loud or visible in protests or mass actions, but they contribute through dialogue and institutional processes, which can be just as important depending on the situation. 10/10 At the end of the day, it really comes down to what kind of leadership students value more: consistent visibility, strategic negotiation, or a mix of both. Looking at both strengths and weaknesses can hopefully lead to more informed choices in the upcoming elections. March 23, 1:44:00 AM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] Hindi ko na mahanap yung entry, anyway, this is about dun sa mga rants na kesho, masungit mga tao sa OSA dahil sa suot, kulay ng buhok, absents and all possible reasons under the sun. Don't get me wrong, ah. Pero parang OA naman na ata na pati pagsita sa mga kulay ng buhok and all, ay irrant pa. I mean come on, buti nga ngayon pwede na magkulay ng hair, or magsuot ng shorts sa pagpasok sa school. Nag adjust na lahat eh. Nung sa time ng mga kuya ko, kahit umuulan, dapat naka pants and shoes. eh ngayon, pwede na sandals and shorts, like dress down and all. Balik tayo dun sa reactions or way ng OSA sa mga 'VIOLATORS', yes ALL CAPS. Come to think of it. You choose to study in [Baguio/Benguet], a [REDACTED_NAME]/Catholic Institution, so meaning my rules yan. I mean, may rules naman sa kahit anong school, pero mas strict lang talaga kapag nasa Catholic school ka. Anyway, so ayun na nga, may rules, why not abide? Sasabihin pa na wala naman sa student handbook, eh hindi ba inisip na "baka bawal" bago gawin? May mga bagay na hindi naman kailangang isulat pa sa handbook para hindi gawin. Let's narrow it, as simple as pagbili ng turon during class hours. Ang paalam ng student iihi, pero rekta cafe luis yan, babalik, may turon. Pero hindi naman nakasulat sa handbook yun, at alam nating mali. Hindi nahuhuli, kasi hindi naman alam ng mga nagbebenta sa canteen kung anong oras ang break ng mga students. Eh, nahuli ka kasi masyadong bright yung hair dye mo. eh alam ng mga guard or sa OSA na bawal yun, eh di pinagsabihan. Going back sa OSA, although never ko pa naexperience masungitan sa OSA na walang reason. Yes, ilang beses na rin akong napatawag sa OSA dahil sa absences, lates, and all. Ang point dito ay hindi naman tayo papupuntahin sa OSA kung walang nakitang violation. And sa masungit, pagalit, padabog, pataas na boses, sana maintindihan natin na ang OSA ang may hawak sa lahat ng student sa [Baguio/Benguet]. Kung tayo ngang students, napapagod, naddraine, to the point na halos isumpa na natin yung ibang profs na sobrang hirap pakisamahan, how much more ang OSA na hawak ang libo-libong studyante na matitigas ang ulo, hindi lahat, pero meron talaga yung student na napagsabihan na minsan, uulitin pa. Kaya kalma lang. understand, reflect, and dapat ggraduate na Louisian. Someday, magiging proud ka na Louisian ka, at tatawanan mo na lang lahat ng nangyari. -Ate mong 3rd year regular, malapit na magtapos, pero mukhang mauunang tapusin ng acads ang eabab na ito March 22, 10:30:06 PM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] Dear A.A. I am writing to reach you - even if each word I put down is one word further away from the truth that I am so desperately trying to hide from you. I am writing because this is the way I express myself- through thousands of words which I go over and over in my head, thinking if this is the right word for this sentence or if I sound okay or just right or if I’m being too over the top. I am writing this letter because I don’t think I will ever have the courage to break whatever we have now. I am writing this letter because I wanted you to know that I am so grateful to have you in my life, and that I love you. We’ve been friends for over a year. Last semester was when I realized I started having feelings for you. I had no clue how it started, but it just hit me one day when we were at our regular spot in Silang 4th floor, when you brushed my hair away because it was covering my eyes as I was trying to write an assignment for MtLaws. It was awkward, I had to admit - how i felt you stare right at me, but I couldn’t help but feel my heart race. It was then I started to think how much I cared for you. You are one of the people in the world who wants to know the deep and real version of me - the other me I don’t show to our Cof because I’m too scared that they’d get tired of me, which is probably the reason why friend “A” told me that I feel “so near yet so far” during my birthday last November. You take initiative to ask if I’m doing okay, or why I feel down and for that, thank you and I love you. One of my favorite memories this semester was when we went out with our Cof late at night and you accompanied me to the comfort room while holding my hand because you remembered that I was scared of walking alone in the dark due to pass incidents. you didn’t care if people stared, even when i tried to pull my hand away you just grabbed onto it tighter. At one point, I began to think what really mattered to me. That was you, you matter to me. You treated me with kindness, love, and understanding. You made me feel safe and warm and I couldn’t ask for a better friend in the world right now, and maybe it’s alright if that’s what we’ll always be, just bros who are there for each other. I still laugh at the thought of writing a confession letter to you this [REDACTED_NAME] 1st where I poured my heart and soul into saying the words I could not say to your face. And i still remember how you thanked me for loving you and how you said you were sorry for making me feel sad and confused. But you acknowledged what I was feeling and made it clear at that time that we could not be something more. It doesn't change anything, but it does make this a little less painful. I mean how could I have possibly thought that you would see me the same way, you’re a straight guy who is madly in love with our friend and I’m a bisexual guy who is madly in love with you. I am writing this letter because I know you’ll never be able to read this because you currently don’t have a facebook account. I am writing this because I just want to let these feelings out without making you feel burdened of a face to face confession. Maybe, by chance that the feelings I have aren’t really of love but something close to familial love or maybe I’m fooling myself because I don’t want to rob you of your future - how you would always talk about how you want to be married in the Philippines and have your own biological child, because those are things I cannot give you. I promised you that I would be with you forever, and that our friendship would never end no matter what, and that is a promise I am willing to keep. Sincerly, N.D. March 22, 9:31:22 PM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] I got flagged dahil sa hair color ko. I didn't mind toning down the hair color kasi gets ko naman if it's too vibrant for their taste, despite having no clear and official statement sa handbook or kahit man lang sa social media pages ng [Baguio/Benguet]. I get what you mean, though. Grabe, ang dehumanizing ng way how that OSA officer handled it. It was just hair color and I didn't even try to justify it or talk back in any way kasi nga I respect naman if they decide na it's too much. Pero the way she talked, e, parang I did something very bad. I never really got why other students say na ang sungit sa OSA. But now, ngayon pang senior na ako, I realized na those claims are sooo true. I'm running for Latin, passed the board, and got 99+ pr sa NMAT, pero that ma'am sa OSA really got me spiraling into depression, crippling anxiety, and deep-seated low self-esteem (not that I ever got better. I was managing these until pinalala niya super). I spent weeks trying to stop myself from that spiral kasi otherwise, I'll be neglecting my responsibilities, which will then trigger a new set of downward spiral lol. All that for a freaking hair color na I communicated naman how sorry I am and that I'll tone it down as soon as my hair can take it. Ang OA, and I'm aware that I will have a hard time after grad if I don't get my shit together, pero my current mental health is what it is. To emphasize, I don't mind the hair color violation part. I just don't think it would kill them to at least be a little bit nicer lalo if the degree of violation wasn't too much. BTW, I've been coloring my hair since freshman days as a way to cope with stress kasi I don't go out that much anyway. It's 'allowed' in my department. This is my first-ever violation of any kind so the treatment is giving me quite a shock. 'Di na lang hinintay bago ako umalis ng [Baguio/Benguet] for good sa May, hays.[Baguio/Benguet] March 21, 12:46:46 AM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] It’s always the ones who proudly flex being a “teacher’s pet” and casually mention beach trips and hangouts with certain profs... that have the most to say about other people’s mistakes. Loud when calling out cheating, but very quiet when it comes to their own track record. Interesting. Some people really act like proximity equals credibility—like being close automatically makes you right, or worse, untouchable. 😶 If you’re going to hold others accountable, at least hold yourself to the same standard. Otherwise, it’s not integrity—it’s just selective outrage. And honestly, nakakatawa na lang yung ibang students na ang lakas mang-call out ng cheating issues. As if naman hindi rin sila gumagawa nun before (or hanggang ngayon lol). Walang ambag lab reports, ang lakas mang-side eye sa iba. Pero kapag sila ang nahuhuli? Smile lang ang prof, tapos okay na ulit. Walang consequences. Hindi ko naman sinasabi na perfect ako—far from it. Pero at least let’s not pretend to be morally superior habang ginagawa rin naman yung same things we criticize. 🥰 At the end of the day, ang unfair lang na may special treatment. Kung may rules, dapat applicable sa lahat. Hindi yung depende sa kung sino ka at gaano ka ka-close. Nakakapagod mag-effort kung ganito ang sistema. March 20, 1:26:45 PM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] Pharmacy 1 MedChemLec students under Sir E. Some of us actually made time to review, walang tulog, at kahit iba pang sacrifices ginawa just to handa sa quizzes—pero kahit ganun, a lot of you still managed to cheat. Our block got called out, and honestly, marami sa inyo e—three friend groups pa ata involved. Not sure kung nag-leak o talagang nag-cheat lang, pero one thing is certain: karamihan sa inyo did cheat. To that one friend group na na-call out na rin here sa Freedom Wall: grabe the audacity. B*bo ba kayo o talagang makapal lang ang mukha? Proud pa kayo sa scores niyo na “perfect,” pero hello, result lang ‘yan ng cheating. Nakakahiya kasi college na kayo, pero ganito pa rin ang behavior. Kung wala kayong hiya sa sarili niyo, kahit sa profs o blockmates niyo na lang sana kayo nahiya. To the other friend group naman, sa ibang course nagp-phone kayo, pero unexpectedly nagawa niyo rin mag-cheat sa MedChemLec, where lahat desididong pumasa. Nakakatawa pa kasi one of you got “lowkey” called out by Sir E— another entry for kahihiyan. Yong mga nag-review nang maayos, nadadamay dahil sa inyo. Nakaka-disappoint, nakaka-galit, at nakakahiya ‘yang kakapalan ng mukha niyo. Medyo vibes vibes lang tayo kay Sir E, pero having a chill relationship with the prof doesn’t give you the right to cheat. Baka feeling niyo “ok lang ‘to, relax lang,” pero no, hindi okay. Every time you cheat, nadadamay ang buong block. Lahat ng blockmates nadadamay sa ginawa niyo, hindi lang kayo. Nakakahiya sa faculty, nakaka-stress sa mga nag-review nang maayos, at nakakagalit sa mga students na talagang desidido pumasa. Funny talaga kasi ilang beses na-mention sa’tin ni Sir kung gaano ka-importante ang integrity together with excellence. Pero after sa cheating incident niyo, ano nga ulit sabi niya? “May excellence oo, pero sa’n ang integrity niyo?” Ang kakapal ng mukha niyo, some of you are literally proud pa sa ginawa niyo, guilt wala? College students na kayo, hindi high schoolers na kailangan pang mag-cheat sheet or even magtanong sa ibang block ano mga sagot sa quiz. At kung sa ibang courses nagcheat kayo, ok fine. Pero sa MedChemLec—course na struggle talaga ng iba—and you still take advantage? Anong ending ngayon? Lahat ng blockmates damay sa ginawa niyo. Wala kayong palya. Sana next time, kahit konting sense of responsibility na lang, huwag niyo na idamay ang iba. March 20, 1:20:35 PM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] An Open Letter to Social Work 3 In all my years of education, I have never experienced an environment as toxic as Social Work 3. Everything feels watched and judged—every movement, every word, even the smallest things. I admit that I am not perfect and that I have also said and done things that were wrong. But this is no longer just about individual mistakes—the environment itself has become the problem. Instead of focusing on learning, it often feels like we are simply trying to survive the pressure and criticism around us. It has reached the point where some of our classmates have already transferred or stopped because of how we treat one another. That alone should make us pause and reflect. Even the professors are, in some ways, part of why this learning environment feels unhealthy. At times, the way concerns are handled, the tone used in addressing students, or the lack of mediation in conflicts can unintentionally add to the pressure rather than ease it. Instead of feeling guided, some of us feel more anxious and hesitant to express ourselves. We are in a Catholic school and are studying Social Work, a field that is built on values like respect, empathy, and compassion. Yet sometimes those values are missing in how we treat each other. If we cannot show basic respect and understanding to our own classmates, how can we expect to serve others in the future? This should be a safe space for us to learn, grow, and support one another—not a place that pushes people away. I hope we all take a moment to reflect. Something needs to change. March 17, 9:38:16 PM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] quick rant lang It’s honestly funny—and disappointing—how some of you are already in college, yet you still choose to cheat. You might say ang hypocrite naman to speak about this, but let’s be real... college na tayo. Ang tagal niyo nang nag-aaral. Wala pa rin bang basic integrity? There’s this friend group sa block namin, and I won’t even hesitate to say which block this comes from—PHARMACY 1C (ifykyk). During exams, you were called out—hindi man by name, pero clear naman kasi sino ba tinignan? Kayo lang din. And hindi lang naman si Sir ang nakapansin. You let your friend sneak a look at your paper—yes, especially you, kuyang tiktoker. And honestly, parang halos every quiz may nac-call out sa group niyo—regardless if minor or major subjects. That alone should’ve been a wake-up call already. During quiz sa isang major natin, you weren’t called out that time simply because you weren’t caught. Sir U trusted us enough to check our own papers, believing we wouldn’t change or add answers—yet dalawa sa fg niyo literally used a black pen to fill in blank answers while checking, aware kayo ro’n pero you guys laughed pa ‘di ba? Meanwhile, a lot of us were devastated because that practical quiz was extremely time-pressured, but we accepted our scores because ano bang magagawa namin kung ‘yon na ‘yon. What makes it even more ironic is that some instructors actually look up sa SONAHBS— they expect honesty during those times—and yet ganiyan kayo. Nakakahiya. This isn’t about being perfect; it’s about having basic decency and respect for your professors, your blockmates, and even the profession you’re preparing to enter. If you can cheat this easily now, what happens when real lives are involved in the future? Tahimik lang ‘yong iba, but that doesn’t mean walang nakakakita. College na tayo. Act like it. [Baguio/Benguet] To my happy crush from BS Pharma, I’ve learned how to like you from a distance. From across the library floors in Vath. From different programs under the same school. From moments where our eyes almost meet but don’t stay long enough to mean anything. From the quiet space between what I feel and what I dare to show. You probably don’t notice me when I arrive daily at the 4th floor earlier than my classes. I tell myself it’s just to study, just to be productive. But truthfully, it’s also because you’re there. Because somehow, that corner of the library feels different when you are. I sit a few tables away, pretending to read, pretending to focus, but my attention drifts. From the way you tuck your hair behind your ear, to the soft seriousness you carry while working. I’ve memorized the rhythm of your presence. And maybe that’s the hardest part — being close enough to see you, but not close enough to know you. Sometimes I wish I were braver. Brave enough to ask about your day. Brave enough to be more than just another face studying under the same lights. Instead, I stay where I’ve always been...a few steps back, a little out of frame, hoping that one day I won’t just be part of the background of your shift. Until then, I’ll keep showing up a little earlier than I need to. Not just to study — but to be near you, even if you never know why. --🍀-- February 23, 1:15:08 AM HKT

I went to the Student Affairs Office to ask about getting excused for upcoming classes due to a family wedding. I didn't know about the school policies on family weddings beforehand. However, what made the experience difficult was how it was communicated. The staff member raised her voice and immediately said “No,” while crossing her arms. Her posture and facial expression (one eyebrow raised) felt intimidating and judgmental, which honestly caught me off guard. The conversation continued in the same tone even after I already acknowledged the policy. I was also asked questions like “Ano ka ba sa kasal?” and when I answered that I was part of the bridesmaids, it was emphasized repeatedly that weddings are considered personal unless it involves immediate family. This was explained while other people were present in the office, and it felt embarrassing because it was loud enough for nearby tables to hear. To be clear, I am not questioning the policy. I understand that rules exist. I’m more concerned about how the interaction was handled and how overwhelming it felt as a student just trying to clarify proper procedures. After leaving the office, I felt physically unwell and very distressed. I just want to ask: Has anyone else experienced something similar when approaching Student Affairs? [Baguio/Benguet] I guess I have to start this differently now. You left. And I still like you, Moao. I guess I'm kind of a creep for holding onto something only I felt. I’m the kind of person who falls easily. I always have been. I fall like it’s instinct, like it’s safer to be in love with the idea of someone than to stand still. And most of the time, I fall out just as easily. It’s a pattern I’ve memorized. A few weeks of intensity, of overthinking every glance, and then I’m back to normal like it never happened. But there have been exceptions. The slow ones. The ones that take their time growing roots. The ones where I don’t realize I’m in too deep until my heart is already betraying me—beating too fast when they’re near, softening at the sound of their name. You were one of those. I tried to tell myself it was just another cycle starting up again. Just me being dramatic. Just a coincidence. But you remembered when I told you how I felt. You asked me about my life like you actually wanted the answers. You did that small, shy wave when we’d see each other. Your bangs falling over your forehead like you didn’t bother to fix them. Your hair in that tight bun, baby hairs at the nape of your neck catching the light. And your laugh—God. That dorky, unfiltered laugh that makes me laugh too, even when I don’t know why. I remember you clearly. The way the air changes when you’re amused. The way my spine straightens and softens at the same time when you say my name. And that’s the problem. You’re gone now, and my world can’t bend around your laughter anymore. It just kept moving. Which feels unfair. You were always out of my league. Whole. Complete. A woman who didn’t need me. And maybe that’s why this hurts the way it does—because I knew, even while I was falling, that I was stepping off something high. Forgive me, for I let it grow anyway. I let myself imagine warmth. Closeness. The sweetness of what could have been, like biting into an apple in the middle of winter and pretending it was summer. I let the roots spread even though I know what happens when I do. I start wanting too much. I start building futures out of glances. And now I’m left holding something that never fully existed. I never even touched you outside my head, so how is it that I still feel like I’ve lost something real? Love is too strong of a word. “Like” is too small. “Admire” feels unfinished. Whatever it was, it lingers. I hoped you wouldn’t vanish. I hoped you wouldn’t turn into something I’d have to remember alone. But here you are—already drifting into that soft, unreachable space in my mind. If you must be a ghost, then at least haunt me gently. Let me keep the way you smiled. The way you said my name. The version of you that existed in those moments. I still like you. I know you don’t feel the same, and I’m not saying this to change that. I just needed you to know. Even in the coldest season Baguio has given me, I still burn for you. Even when you’re not here. Even when there’s nothing left to hold onto. It’s quiet now. No expectations. No hope trying to be bigger than it should be. Just this feeling. -Crtw (submitted this somewhere else just know it's me) February 12, 9:30:23 PM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] Pasakay lang sa [Baguio/Benguet] This also happened to me way back . I was so out of shape during my last year na i often faint dahil sa stress at fatigue(i was anemic pala). May time dati ba naka pacheck up ako, was prescribed medicine na sabi sa 2nd floor kunin ko sa baba and mag final check up doon sa doctor doon, when she saw yung reseta, she said di ko naman daw need mga yun, mag vitamins na lang daw ako. So i went home sa province namin that weekend para magpa check up kasi saktong wala pasok nun, sinundo ako ng parents ko para magpa check sa family doctor namin kasi ang hirap din mag sched ng appointment noon sa BGH The fainting spells lessened pero it still happened occasionally, may time na i was walking papuntang school(taga bakakeng pa ako), and bandang malcolm square eh i blacked out for quite a while and people helped me naman, so sabi ko since may time naman ako magpa check up, punta na lang ulit ako da clinic. When i got there, may pila, and naubos oras ko sa pila, i saw my prof nung naka pila ako and said na i wasnt feeling well and he understood, basta ipakita ko na lang daw yung cert na manggagaling sa clinic, when i got there sabi di naman daw ako nahimatay sa school premises and i looked ok naman na daw kaya wala sila magagawa, hindi rin ako binigyan ng med cert kasi nga daw di naman ako sa school nahimatay(which kinda negates yung rule nila na pag may sakit ka, and need mo medcert, and acceptable lang is either galing sa kanila or magpa check up ka privately) Then there was a time din na i was feeling really sick, like ang putla ko na daw tignan sabi ng mga classmates ko kaya they insisted na pumunta akong clinic(which i was discouraged na dahil sa past scenarios, pero pumunta pa rin ako just in case). [Baguio/Benguet] SUBJECT: AYAW KO NA. YOU REMOVED BS MATH SA [Baguio/Benguet], AND NOW YOU ARE LOBOTOMIZING COMPUTER SCIENCE. ​Gusto ko lang naman mag-aral. My dream was to take BS Math, pero ano ginawa ng administration years ago? YOU REMOVED THE PROGRAM. Why? Because "too few students enroll"? So you sacrificed academic purity for profit? Dahil walang pera sa Math, tinanggal niyo? ​So I was forced to settle for Computer Science. Sabi ko sa sarili ko, "Okay lang, CS is basically Applied Math. At least I'll get rigorous training in logic and computation." ​I WAS WRONG. I WAS SCAMMED. ​1. THE FIRST TWO YEARS ARE A TOTAL WASTE OF LIFE. Dalawang taon. DALAWANG TAON akong naghintay ng "Science" sa Computer SCIENCE. Freshman and Sophomore years should be the "weeding out" phase—puno dapat ng rigorous theory, Calculus, and Physics. Buti pa nga sa [Baguio/Benguet] may physics pa sila e! Instead, you flooded us with Software Development courses immediately! Intro to Computing? Web Dev? Programming 1 & 2? You are training us to be WEB DEVELOPERS and IT PROFESSIONALS, not Computer Scientists! You are obsessed with producing "employable" graduates who can churn out websites, but you are neglecting the students who want to be Researchers and Architects! By the time we reach 3rd year, bobo na kami sa Math kasi kinalawang na yung utak namin kakagawa ng HTML/CSS/GUI/XML at Java CRUD apps! ​2. THERE IS BARELY ANY MATH. IT’S A DESERT. Let’s get this straight: Computer Science is Applied Mathematics. You cannot separate the two. Pero anong curriculum 'to? ​Nasaan ang Calculus 1-3? ​Nasaan ang Linear Algebra? ​Nasaan ang Abstract Algebra? ​Nasaan ang Advanced Statistics? ​Ang meron lang tayo: Discrete Math, "Mathematics for CS," at Numerical Methods. That’s it? For a 4-year course? YOU ARE STARVING US. ​3. THE "SPECIALIZATION" TRAP (DATA SCIENCE & AI WITHOUT MATH??) And now, I look at the prospectus for our 3rd Year. May subjects na Data Science, AI, and Machine Learning. Nakakatawa. Nakakaiyak. HOW THE FUCK ARE YOU GOING TO TEACH US MACHINE LEARNING/ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE WHEN YOU DIDN'T TEACH US CALCULUS/Differential Equations AND LINEAR ALGEBRA?! ICRACRAM NALANG BA LAHAT LAHAT NETO SA NUMERICAL METHODS??? ​I opened a real Machine Learning textbook (Goodfellow, Bishop, Sutton). It is PURE MATH. It is gradients, heavy statistics, and matrix operations from scratch. Pero dahil wala kaming math foundation, alam ko na mangyayari. You will teach AI like Software Engineering. You won't teach us how it works; you will just teach us how to import pytorch or import tensorflow. You are training us to be API Callers, not Scientists! We will be "using" the AI, not "building" it. Para tayong nag-aaral ng mechanical engineering pero tinuruan lang tayo mag-drive imbes na gumawa ng makina! ​4. COMPUTER ARCHITECTURE: THE TECHNICIAN JOKE. Ito yung midterms namin: Tinuruan kaming mag-disassemble ng PC. WHAT THE FUCK? Computer Science students kami, hindi kami technician sa Gilmore! Bakit kami nagbabaklas ng PC parts para sa midterms?! ​Where is Pipelining? ​Where is Instruction Set Architecture (RISC vs CISC)? ​Where is Memory Hierarchy and Cache Mapping? This is supposed to be about the logic of the machine! Tapos ang final project, ARDUINO? Are you kidding me? While Arduinos are fun, that is Embedded Systems/IoT, NOT Computer Architecture! We should be simulating processors or writing Assembly/Verilog, not wiring sensors like a High School Science Fair! STOP TREATING US LIKE TECHNICIANS. ​5. OPERATING SYSTEMS: THE ULTIMATE INSULT. Sa UP, sa MIT, sa Stanford, at sa ibang [REDACTED_NAME] League schools, ang project sa OS is building a Kernel, writing a Process Scheduler, or creating a Shell from scratch (like the Pintos project). They learn how the OS manages the hardware. ​Dito satin? "Gagawa ng Android App." PUTANGINA, ANONG KINALAMAN NG PAGGAWA NG ANDROID APP SA OPERATING SYSTEMS?! That is Application Development. That is sitting on top of the OS. The OS subject exists to teach us what happens underneath! Binabayaran namin 'tong subject na 'to para matuto ng Concurrency, Deadlocks, Paging, at Virtual Memory HINDI LANG SA LECTURE, PATI RIN SA LAB. Hindi para gumawa ng isa na namang eCommerce app na kaya namang gawin ng kahit sinong nanood ng Indian YouTube tutorial! ​6. HCI IS JUST "APP DEV PART 1". Human-Computer Interaction should be about Cognitive Psychology, Fitts' Law, and Usability Research. Tinuro naman to sa lecture, PERO parang filler lamang mga lessons na ito! Pero anong ginagawa namin? "Gagawa ulit ng Application Prototype." Ano bayan, gusto ko sana magresearch ng effects ng social media sa generation na to kasi nabrabrainrot na mga kabataan at puro nalang sila cellphone. Magreels magdamag. Or yung magexperiment sana kami like psychologists who observe people using technology. Tapos yung prototype na 'to, ito yungico-code sa OS na gagawint ANDROID APP? So you literally turned three different subjects (Arch, HCI, OS) into one giant, redundant "Make a Mobile App" loop! OK NAMAN YUNG LECTURE which is focused naman about sa subject na to, pero yun nga hindi siya rigorous, puro memorize lang. Yung lab namin yung nakakabwisit kasi ang mahal ng lab fee at ANG BAGAL NG WIFI! ​7. THE LABORATORY TUITION SCAM. And let's talk about the money. Nagbabayad kami ng malaking LABORATORY FEES, pero anong nakukuha namin? SELF-STUDY. Papasok si Prof -> Bibigay ng PDF/Activity -> "Gawin niyo yan, ipasa before 5PM" -> Prof sits down and does nothing. WE ARE PAYING YOU TO TEACH US, NOT WATCH US. We can find tutorials on YouTube for free! Kaya kami nag-enrol para turuan kami ng best practices, bigyan kami ng complex examples, at i-guide kami kung bakit nag-eerror yung logic namin. Hindi yung hahayaan lang kaming mangapa tapos magche-check lang kayo ng output. Teach us the "Why," not just the "How." ​​8. I WANT TO BE A QUANT, NOT JUST A PROGRAMMER! Hindi lahat ng CS student gustong maging Web Developer. Some of us want to be Quantitative Analysts. We want to build financial models, high-frequency trading algorithms, and risk management systems. Some of us want to be Researchers in Theoretical Computer Science—yung tipong inaaral ang P vs NP problem, computational complexity, at cryptography. ​Pero paano ko maaabot yun ​Nasaan ang Stochastic Calculus? ​Nasaan ang Brownian Motion? ​Nasaan ang Advanced Probability Theory? Tangina parang sapat nalang ba ang statistics na tinuro sa GMATH, di nga tinuro yung proof ng central limit theorem. ​Imbes na ituro sa amin ang math behind market volatility or complex simulations, anong ginagawa namin? ACTIVITY DIAGRAMS. USE CASE DIAGRAMS. DOCUMENTATION Paulit-ulit na "System Analysis and Design" na puro drawing ng boxes at arrows! MY GOD, I AM TIRED OF DRAWING BOXES. You are obsessed with the process of documentation, but you completely ignore the mathematical engines that should be running inside the software! ​9. THE TRAGEDY OF OUR PROFESSORS. Nakakadurog ng puso makita yung mga Top-Notch Math Professors natin. These are brilliant minds—experts in Abstract Algebra, Topology, and Analysis—and what is the department forcing them to do? Pinipilit niyo silang magturo ng boring-ass Software Development courses. OUR PROF THERE WAS AN [Metro Manila] MATHEMATICS GRADUATE FFS. Sayang ang talino nila! You are forcing mathematicians to teach "Intro to Android" or generic IT subjects just to fill a loading. It’s an insult to their expertise and a disservice to us students who are starving for their actual knowledge. ​To the Administration: You killed BS Math because it wasn't "profitable." Now you are killing Computer Science by turning it into IT. COMPUTER SCIENCE IS NOT SOFTWARE ENGINEERING AND DEFINITELY IS A DIFFERENT FIELD COMPARED TO IT. Stop training us to be "employable monkey coders" for boring CRUD apps. Train us to be Scientists. Train us to be Quants. Train us to be researchers. Train us to be mathematicians. ​IBALIK niyo na po yung BS Math curriculum, o ayusin niyo ang ComSci please lang. Gustong gusto ko na magshift. I tried my best na maging loyal sa [Baguio/Benguet] KASI dun ako nagkaroon ng maraming kaibigan saka andun yung bestfriend ko. Nameet ko rin si [PROFESSOR_NAME] na mathematics graduate rin from [Baguio/Benguet] and highly inspired of him for his passion of teaching mathematics not as a tool, but as a discovery. So please lang admin. Fix the curriculum. Bring back mathematics. Im desperate. Simula nung bata palang ako, alam ko na math ang gusto ko. Ngayon diko na alam. February 9, 8:39:48 PM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] Since there have been many discussions regarding the [Baguio/Benguet] Clinic, I would also like to share my experience. While these are personal observations, I have noticed that similar concerns have been raised by other students as well. I hope these points can be recognized as areas for improvement and serve as a basis for possible amendments in institutional policies and services. I am open to hearing other perspectives as well, as this is meant to encourage constructive discussion. 🙂 📍 1. Inefficient queuing and waiting system The current lining process can be very inconvenient, especially for students with limited vacant hours. Waiting for 1–2 hours for a consultation, and sometimes longer due to additional queues upstairs, can be difficult to manage alongside academic responsibilities. While students are expected to plan ahead, a more efficient appointment or queuing system may help reduce delays and improve accessibility. 📍2. Lack of approachability and patient-centered communication Another concern is the manner in which some staff interact with students upon entering the clinic. The questioning can sometimes feel more interrogative than supportive, which may leave patients feeling anxious or invalidated. Although medical protocols and standards are necessary for proper diagnosis and monitoring, kindness and professionalism in tone and word choice can greatly affect the comfort of students who are already unwell. 📍 3. Difficulty in obtaining medical certificates or assessments Illness is often unpredictable. Students may wake up with symptoms such as fever or dizziness and may initially take medication and rest before seeking consultation. However, some students report being told that they should have visited the clinic while symptoms were at their peak in order to receive proper documentation. This can be challenging, as it may require students to leave their dorms while actively sick and wait in long lines simply to obtain proof of illness. While it is understandable that diagnoses require observable and measurable symptoms, students should still be met with understanding, especially since not everyone has access to private healthcare providers and may rely solely on school services for medical certification. It is also fair to acknowledge the possibility of misuse, but this should be balanced with the reality that many students genuinely seek medical care and support. [Baguio/Benguet] Clinic, I sincerely hope that service delivery can become more efficient, patient-centered, and compassionate, for the welfare of all students. 🙂 February 9, 5:32:03 PM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] Not really [Baguio/Benguet] related pero for awareness Our dog passed away two days ago because a careless tourist ran over him at a sharp curve, it was to a point na di namalayan ng driver na natamaan niya dog namin, thats how fast she was driving, kinailangan pa siya habulin ng mga tao just to make her stop. Our dog in less than a minute stopped breathing tapos andami pang dugo. She was brought to the station hoping to settle things with her, kaso apparently its our fault dahil di daw nakatali aso namin, for context; mag iihaw daw kasi mga kapatid ko and they had the back of the van open where our dog was sat, parang garage siya na open, tapos my sister got out the house and when our dog saw that he jumped off the van and ran to her, dun na nangyari nasagasaan siya. Imagine if bata na nagtatawid tawid yon sa neighborhood diba, sharp curve yun bat ambilis bilis magpatakbo, alangan itali mo din yung bata. Going back sa police station, she was insisting na 12 lang takbo nila, 12 takbo di makakailag aso don? The girl na nakasaga had no remorse at all wala daw kahit sorry, mas important pa daw para sakanya yung night market nila that they were on their way to, nagpakavictim pa siya mentioning how her mental health could get affected by this. Tapos not sure ha cause i wasnt there pero my other sister saw the police and the girl exchanging some papers tapos they even talked in private “istg na bribe na yun” kasi the girl and her family sobrang yaman daw talaga tignan covered in jewelry, latest iphones, tapos a really nice car. My sister was asking for compensation, okay na yung cremation and ultrasound ng other dog namin na pregnant with the kids of our dog na nasagasaan. Sabat daw ng sabat yung police na hindi need magbayad nung nakasagasa kasi daw siya liable etc, pero thats common human decency na diba, nakapatay ka na wala ka padin paki? In the end ang nakuha lang namin is 5k and thats just for cremation. So this is an awareness post to keep your pets nalang indoors kasi the ordinance for dogs in Baguio sucks, pag pinamukha nilang napatay nila accidentally aso mo sa daan magiging kasalanan mo pa. February 9, 4:27:26 PM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] I just saw a rant regarding the clinic services, and I want to share my experience as well. I can’t help but notice the huge difference between the [Baguio/Benguet] clinic and the clinic from my high school (which also catered to college students). I had my work immersion at our high school clinic, and one thing I learned there is that they never allow patients to wait for an hour. In fact, staff would get upset if the line took too long because they knew patients were already uncomfortable or unwell. They were also very empathetic and understanding toward whatever condition the patient had. Here in [Baguio/Benguet], the waiting time is honestly exhausting. Even when the line is short, it still takes 1–2 hours. Why are students who are already seeking medical attention made to wait that long? Why is there only one person doing the interview, causing everything to move even slower? And during the interview, you can clearly see from their facial expressions that they seem doubtful of what the student is reporting—as if we’re not being believed. I personally experienced this when I consulted for dysmenorrhea. The doctor initially misunderstood and immediately asked about a medical certificate because I mentioned I needed one. The nurse then explained that a med cert requires a 24-hour observation and asked if I had missed any subjects. I said yes, because that was the reason I needed it. The nurse responded that the consultation should have been before the subject, then added “oh diba” after repeating my condition—said in a way that made it feel like I was lying. What I don’t understand is this policy of needing a consultation before the class when the consultation itself takes so long because of the waiting time. The class I missed was from 7:30–10:30, and given the clinic process, how exactly do they expect students to manage this? Students don’t go to the clinic for fun—we go because we’re in pain, sick, or genuinely need help February 9, 12:41:53 AM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] Since I'm seeing numbers of "[Baguio/Benguet] OSA stories" here, I'd like to share mine too. This was around during short term. May Gmath sub ako noon. Always present ako pero nagkasakit one time kaya hindi nakapasok. Nawala sa isip ko yung pagkuha ng excuse slip that time kasi medyo busy dahil kahit 3 subs lang sa short term eh andami activities at halos everyday pa ang quizzes. I got INC sa Gmath kasi I missed 1 quiz. Naasikaso ko lang yung excuse slip ko nung enrollment na for the next sem/sy. Nagpunta ako sa OSA dala ang excuse letter signed by a parent and fillout ng excuse slip. [Baguio/Benguet] CLINIC DO BETTET OR SHUT DOWN. Buti bumabalik si freedom wall, di ko alam kelan to mauupload kasi naghahabol pa sila ng mga posts from pero gusto ko parin i share since naging active ulit yng FW. Anyways, My first encounter for sa clinic pumasok ako sa school pero nagka acid ako sumabay pa sa period ko and sobrang sakit kaya naisip ko sa clinic na since nasa school naman ako. 10:00am ako nakapila tapos by 10:30 nag cut off sila. Walang sinabi sa mga studyanteng naka pila, nagbigay lang ng paper sa isang student na sabe cut off na after siya. Ako naman sobrang frustrated kasi sa haba nung pila (15-20 students cguro and at student 5 sila nagbigay nung cut off paper) nakatayo ako sa 30mins na yon na sana ginamit ko nalang para makauwi and magpa clinic nalang malapit sa bahay. Tapos 10:30am palang, bakit nag cu cut off na sila? Pumunta na ako sa OSA kasi sobrang sakit na ng tyan ko at puson ko di ko alam yung area kaya don ako nagpatulong. Sila ate din sabi OSA nabigla sila nung sinabe ko nag cut off na sila kasi hindi dapat pwede yon and sabe nalang nila na punta ako sa clinic sa may t.alonzo, tiniis ko nalang kasi wala naman ako magagawa sobrang frustrated nalang ako at naiiyak na sa sakit pero iniisip ko din yng mga mamiss ko na activities and quizzes na need ko ng med cert. 2nd time was [REDACTED_NAME] na kasi ever since then ayoko na sa [Baguio/Benguet] clinic pero ngayon naman biglang bumalik yng pain ko (got diagnosed last year) ngayon lang siya nangyari na nasa school naman ako kaya sinubukan ko ulit bigyan ng chance ulit yung clinic (wala rin akon pera for clinic at the time). Mabilis lang yung pagprocess nila sakin kasi naiyak na ako sa sakit pero it does not make up for what i experienced while waiting to be processed inside while crying in pain. Nag brown out ng mga 1min cguro kaya pagpasok ko nung clinic down pa yung net nila. Yung mga nurse(?) or attending staff naman don sa harapan ko mismo nagjo joke na silang ayaw na nilang tumanggap ng studyante at mag cut off nalang kaya sila (9:30am palang yng oras) tapos tumatawa nalang sila na iniisip na lahat nung mga nakapila ay babalik nalang bukas. Nag joke din sila na "baka may tataas na naman dito kasi nareport tayo". So nareport naman na pala kayo sa behavior niyo. Wala rin kayong compassion sa mga studyanteng nasa labas na pumipila magpa check up. What about the students who pushed themselves to come to school para lang magpa check up sainyo tapos bigla bigla nalang kayo nagcucut off? Do better. Be better. February 8, 9:27:40 PM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] Silent Confession ni (M) Kilala mo ba ang pakiramdam? The slow, crushing realization that you've spent years as a ghost, haunting circles you longed to belong to? My life was a performance of people-pleasing, driven by a deep, dark fear: ang takot na mawala ang mga alaala. I became a different person, just to simply belong. I knew my flaws. I was hyper-aware of every single flaw, trying desperately to file them down, but I only ended up spiraling back. Kapag sinubukan kong maging ako, it felt like I had offended everyone. I had friends, pero pakiramdam ko hindi ako kabilang. I’d change personalities on the fly, exhausted, only to wake up at 3 a.m. sharp with the silence screaming: Okay pa ba sa kanila ang presensya ko? This is the truth of it: sometimes, the happiest person is the loneliest. Their mind is a cage, where they hurt themselves to protect everyone else. Kaya guys, they leave, knowing their forced happiness will eventually fade. I went through that. I walked away, heavy with guilt. I needed them to tell me what I did wrong. I needed that verdict to change, but there was only silence. That's why I left, I never wanted to blame them even if I speak so ill of them. To me, the memories of what I had mattered kahit nasaktan ako ilang beses. And now, the urge is back. Ang pagnanais na umalis ulit and change every single piece of myself, just so I can finally be part of someone's story (grabe, nagiging side character). But if I did decide to walk away this time, I truly don't think anyone will ask where I went. The only thing they might carry is a sudden, sharp realization when they understand just how much I meant to them, and how much I will always choose their well-being over my own. Ito yung confession ko so if you know me... Please just smile for me and pretend it's normal, I don't want this burden to affect you as well. December 4, 2:38:37 PM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] Hello! I just wanted to rant a bit and share my experience with the university's handling of certain procedures in hopes of informing others in a similar situation. Keep in mind na this happened a few weeks ago from now. Context: I was looking to transfer from my previous school and incquired at the student affairs office. I was told to provide some requirements (credentials, 2x2, etc.). This was the first time I asked if it was possible for a transferee to retake a previously failed subject. The answer that I was given was, "possible po." So I proceeded to work on gathering my requirements. I'm sure na a lot of my fellow students know na hindi basta basta lang nakukuha yung mga ganyan na papers. It's a lenghty, timely, and costly process that really takes a toll on you. Especially as a student on a tight budget. Once I had all of the needed requirements, I submitted it and was allowed to fill out my form, take an interview, credit my classes, and await a phone call in order for me to know when I should take the exam and where. Throughout each step, I repeatedly asked if it was possible for a student in my situation to be accepted and retake my failed subject. Each time, I was told that it was possible and I was allowed to proceed to the next step. After a few days I received a call from the office confirming that I would be taking the exam on May 26. We ended the call and only a few hours later I received another call where they asked me to meet them later that afternoon for them to explain something. I go to the SAO the next day and was informed that not only was I not allowed to take the exam but I was also not able to enroll for any other course with a board exam for the very same reason I was constantly worried and asked about. The failed sub. I explained that from the very beginning I've informed them about it and the response I get is "Sorry." Kase late lang nila narealize na hindi pala pwede??? ...huh? Kailangan pa ba na umakyat sa dean bago niyo mapansin na may conflict? As a professional. A guide. As a HANDLER of these important processes...were you not aware of this from the very beginning or were you simply just nodding along para lang matapos yung trabaho mo? Hayyy I am fully aware that big universities have a standard they must hold their students up to. However, by those standards, it is also fair that students expect a high level of professionalism and handling of cases like these. I am not upset about the rejection. I am not even upset about all of the expenses that reached over 2k (not including transpo, etc.) and time spent on gathering things like the necessary papers and effort spent walking from building to building just to fill out forms and collect said papers. I am upset that I was allowed to continue until the very end only to be told the news, with the lack of professionalism as I was constantly spoken to in a condescending tone and backhanded comments, with me being allowed to proceed to only be met with "sorry" after everything. This experience has been one of the absolute worst I have had in terms of handling. So with that being said, to incoming freshmen or transferees, ALWAYS ask questions. Kahit paulitulit pa yan. Keep. Asking. Questions. So as to not waste your time like I did mine at para alam mo din kung all good ka ba or may kailangan kang iprepare. Wag kayong matakot ipa confirm kung tama ba yung sinabi sayo ng staff or hindi. To the Louisians, stay strong! Keep fighting! And goodluck. 💕 May 28, 10:10:45 PM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] Healing from the things we don't deserve is hard, even if we have already accepted them and continue to move on with our lives. Although I've been doing fine these past few months, all the memories suddenly reappear. This haunts me, and I feel so down because no matter how much I assure myself that it's okay to let it out and feel everything, no matter how much I tell myself that it's okay now, that it's done, you're free, you learned, it feels like everything isn't enough. I'm tired of overthinking, weary of letting go, and tired of these feelings. Regardless, I deserved all the love regardless of what I had experienced. I hope we can all heal from the things that traumatized us and from the things we can't tell. I really hope that one day I can wake up and be free like it's really nothing. May 24, 9:13:42 PM HKT

Umiikot-ikot na naman tayo sa parehong lumang tugtugin—sa isang lumang tugtuging gasgas at tila ba’y paulit-ulit na lamang. Pinaglalaruan ang mga tao, nilalaro ang politika, at binabastardya ang hustisya. Isang kamangmangan ang siyang umalulong kamakailan mula sa bibig ng isang senador na gungGong “Ang importante may pagakin sa hapagkainan, matibay na healthcare sa needs ng may sakit, disenteng trabaho, dekalidad na edukasyon, panatag at ligtas na makakauwi sa gabi–hindi naman nakakain itong impeachment .” Umiikot na naman ang mga palusot. Huwag na raw ituloy ang impeachment, marahil daw sa mga nakaabang na panukala at sa mga suliraning hindi pa nareresolba. Pero kailan pa naging hadlang ang katotohanan sa paggawa ng tama? Huwag natin hayaang apakan ng maitim na usapang politikal ang sigaw ng karaniwang Pilipino. Hindi tayo bulag. Hindi tayo pipi. Hindi tayo tanga. At ang Senado, sa pamumuno ni Chiz Escudero, ay tila ba nakikiayon sa pagsisinungaling at pagbura ng katotohanan! Ibinalik nila ang impeachment complaint sa Mababang Kapulungan, kahit malinaw na TALIWAS ito sa Saligang Batas! Isa itong garapal na pag-iwas sa pananagutan! Ito ay tahasang pagtataksil sa bayan! Managot ang dapat na managot, panagutin ang korap na si [REDACTED_NAME] Duterte! Hindi siya reyna. Hindi siya ligtas sa mata ng batas. Dapat siyang singilin, litisin, at parusahan sa mga kasalanan niya sa bayan. ITIGIL ANG PALPAK NA IMPUNIDAD! IPAGLABAN ANG HUSTISYA! Ang Senado ay hindi palaruan ng mga trapo, lalo’t higit ang pamahalaan ay hindi kanlungan ng mga korap! HUSTISYA PARA SA BAYAN! HUSTISYA PARA SA MASA!

[Baguio/Benguet] Additional comment to the “Shouldn’t a Teacher... Teach?” post because I forgot to mention this: One time, during class, one of my classmates broke down in tears while the instructor was, yet again, just reading from her paper instead of actually engaging us in discussion as we took notes as quickly as possible. My classmate cried because of her distress to what was happening in class (nakakapressure rin kasi mag take note kasi and bilis talaga yung pagkabasa ni ma’am). After a few minutes, she tried to return to the room. But as soon as the teacher saw her through the glass window thingy on the door, she stood up and locked it. Medyo natakot ako sa part na ‘to, actually. Then she said, “Hindi nagpaalam eh.” Two of my classmate’s friends told the instructor that she had a breakdown. So, of course, everyone can assume that that means she wasn’t in the right state of mind to ask for permission earlier. Despite that, the instructor stayed silent. That kind of silent anger, especially from someone in a position of authority, can be as intimidating as yelling. Nakakatakot lang talaga yung silence niya. And to the girl who commented on the original post saying: “Instructors" - instruct, "teachers" - teach, sorry to say this but it is the reality ever since. Advice as an ate alumni, let's strive harder on our own and ask our instructor more. Let's give them the benefit of the doubt. I appreciate your intention to give advice. But here’s the thing: we already strive harder than most people think. We study, we do our own research, we help each other out, we ask questions. Just because this kind of system has been the “reality ever since” doesn’t mean we should continue to accept it in silence. A broken reality doesn’t get better by brushing it off, it improves when people speak up and demand change, even in merely little ways. So no, we’re not complaining just because we want to. May 24, 4:25:58 AM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] You ever get that feeling that you don’t belong in your own “friend” group? That’s me right now. It’s a different kind of loneliness, y’know? The feeling of being expendable, of being someone who will not be missed. I always liked to believe that I was good at making friends. Maybe at some point I was. Well, not anymore. Not after everything that happened. I got myself stuck in this purgatory inside a friend group that I don’t really feel any close connection to. A friend group that is very responsible, very organized and are composed of overall good people. In terms of academics and university life, I would say they’re a really good influence. But when push comes to shove, there are qualities in many members that I am not comfortable with. I don’t like the constant mood swings. The constant negative hatred towards other people. The pressure of keeping up academically. It’s suffocating. That’s not even the worst part. I am always the last one to be considered for anything we do. An afterthought, a ghost and a simple add-on. I am left out on many topics, not approached for anything, and not even someone relevant enough to be part of the conversation. Sure, I throw my jokes, my quips and my charms to the table. But sometimes, I get tired of trying to impress people who don’t really see the value in me as a genuine friend. Just someone who’s valuable to be around with. Or at least thats how I feel. Thats how their actions made me feel. “Why won’t you just leave”, you might think. It’s university. You have to be realistic about these things. I hold no ill will towards the friend group personally. I doubt they realize what I feel. I doubt they’d care much. Semester is over, and I am glad to be done with pretending. Just a few more years and I can finally ghost. Start a new thing someplace else, and get to know other people that will care about me. Till then, I have my anonymous freedom wall rants to keep me afloat. Have a great vacation everyone. May 22, 11:13:40 PM HKT
