[Baguio/Benguet] Hi gwani 🧸, I don’t know if you’ll ever read something like this. I’m not even sure I’d ever want to send it. But I think I just need to say it — not for you, but for me. That day we saw each other — it felt like stepping into a version of time that had paused and waited. But we weren’t the same. We were different. Older. Softer in some ways, more distant in others. And yet, there were things in that moment that made me feel like we hadn’t really let go — not completely. You initiated the conversations. You told stories. You remembered things that, honestly, I didn’t expect you to still carry — like that number. The one only you knew. The one you didn’t even need to check twice because you already had it memorized. That moment hit me hard. Not because I forgot about the number, but because I realized... you hadn’t. And I saw it in your silence, in your hands, in your eyes avoiding mine. You were carrying something too. Maybe confusion, maybe old feelings, maybe just the ache of remembering that there was a time when we were everything to each other. When I reflexively reached out to pull you out of the way of that tricycle, I didn’t think. My body just moved. Because even after all this time, some part of me still reacts to protect you. You shrugged me off — and I get it now. It wasn’t rejection. It was distance. Maybe protection. Maybe a boundary you needed to keep between us. But that touch... even for a second, it reminded me that I’m still wired to care. And maybe that’s okay, even if nothing else comes of it. I don’t hate you. I never did. And I don’t regret what we were. Even if it ended. Even if it got messy. Even if it still hurts sometimes when I least expect it to. Maybe in another version of our story, we’d meet again under different circumstances. One where we could look at each other without the weight of what we’ve lost. But this time? It felt like goodbye, even if neither of us said it. So here’s me saying it now: Thank you for showing up that day. Thank you for being honest in the little ways you could. And thank you — even in silence — for still holding on to parts of me for so long. But I’m letting go now. Not because I want to forget you. But because I finally can. — P P.S. A girl from [Metro Manila], a soul from [Baguio/Benguet] — maybe we were always meant to meet halfway, but never stay. May 22, 8:35:35 PM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] Sa lahat ng kirot at pasakit na dulot, Matinding lungkot kung walang gusot. Pinili ko na lang mapag-isa sa buhay, Kaya ngayon ay tila humihingang patay. Hinangad noon na ibsan ng hirap, Siyang dahilan kaya hindi na naghanap; Gayong walang maasahan sa pag-irog, Puso lamang ay tuluyang madudurog! Subalit sa sadyang ayaw magmahal, Bakit may kulang sa loob na tumatagal? Mga karanasang hindi na lang lumitaw, At pumalit ang dagim sa halip na araw. Sa pag-iwas ko sa higit pang trauma, Lalong humapdi ang salat kong dama. May hangaring marubdob upang tuklasin, Kaso damdamin ay pinilit sugpuin. Takot na maulit ang nakaraan, Hindi pinagbigyan ang kinabukasan. Kung sarili sana ay hindi pinagkaitan, Ang ligaya sana ay 'di pinagsisihan! [Baguio/Benguet] Pinagmulan ay kamuntik nang talikuran; Dismaya at pinsalang kung bakit ayaw balikan. Pagbalik ko naman sa ating kabataan, Tinanggal mo ang magiging alinlangan. Pamumuna nila sa aki'y nagpalugmok; Pinagsabihan hanggang sa nagmukmok. Datapwa't dahil sa mahinhin mong tinig, Ako'y napabuti at umalab ang sigasig. Dawis ko'y kanilang pinapalala, Nakadagdag pa dahil minaliit ang problema. Subalit tuwing tayo'y magkasama, Nananariwa ang pananampalataya. Sinasabat ang lahat kong pahiwatig: Ayaw pakinggan ang aking panig. Ngunit dahil sa iyong pakikinig, Ako'y napanatag at napaibig. Kutis mong malambot, buhok mong umaagos, Sa aking damdamin ay tumatagos. Ngunit walang dama kung iba ang humaplos, Sapagkat aking puso'y sa'yo lamang ibinubuhos. Pwede manligaw, aking kaulayaw; At nang ika'y makakasama araw-araw? Ayos lang kung sa ngayo'y umayaw; Basta't sisikapin ko at hindi bibitaw! Sa kabila ng alanganing pag-antabay, Pag-ibig ko'y tapat at walang kapantay. Hangad kong kahit sa yaring buhay, Tayo'y magkasama, masaya, at magkaugnay. Title: Damang Agos Kong Bulaklakan Poem Written by: Ace Jacus May 21, 10:29:46 PM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] May mga tao na kung makamando, Akala'y maganda ang uri ng pagkatao. Didiktahan ka at hahanapan ng mali, Bagaman hindi naman makita ang sarili. Papangalanan ka at nang mahiya, Kung tama ang ginawa... baliwala! Sisimangot kung makatingin sa iyo, Anumang pakisama ay walang panalo. Sa tono pa lang, halatang maldita! Makisalamuha'y laging intrimitida. Sa pula sa puti, hanap lagi'y kakampi, Plastikan at laging sobrang mapanisi. Nangingibabaw sa kawalan ng modo, Walang kapantay sa pagkabulatsero. Sa pagtatangi ng ilan niyang gusto, At pagsasantabi ng ayaw nito. Barumbada, antipatika, suplada, Inuuna ang sarili, siya raw laging tama. Bakit pa aasang may susunod, Kung ang ugali'y nakakapagod? Magkukunwaring maganda ang pakita, Hindi pa rin matikom ang paninita. Satsat, dakdak, dikdik, at utos, Wala ba 'tong alam kung paano magtuos? 'Di ba nahalata na ang nagkulang, Hindi ang sutil at makulit lamang, Kundi ba sa pagiging isang matapobre, Iyon kaya walang nakikinig at may pake? Mag-isip muna bago tayo magsalita, Marahil ay tatawagin ka pang maganda. Bigyang konsiderasyon ang mga sensitibo, Hindi lang naman ikaw ang nasa mundo. Kung paano mo tratuhin ang nahihirapan, Ay siyang batayan ng iyong kinabukasan. Hindi kabawasan ang makibagay, Kaysa maging napakaarte at mataray. Ang buhay ay 'di lamang taasan ng talino, Kundi pati pakikipagkapwa-tao! Kung hanggang dunong ang pananagumpay, Diyos huwag po sanang mag-isa kang mamatay. Title: Ibababa Ang Nagtataas Poem Written by: Ace Jacus May 21, 10:28:51 PM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] Sa paaralan, 'di batayan ang karunungan, Kundi ano lamang nakasaad sa katuruan! Mataas man ang puntos, wala ring natutuhan, Sayang pa ang oras sa walang katuturan. Eh, puro pamumuna ang makukuha mo, Pagkatapos pang mamasahe sa malayo. Iirapan pa ang 'di makahabol sa leksiyon, Ngunit 'di kakayahin sa lahat ng panahon. Lalo na't kung halos tanggalin na ang libangan, Para lamang maglaan ng oras sa kalakaran. Sandamakmak ang gawaing inatasan; Hindi na ito nakabubuti sa kalusugan. Utak na bugbog, isip na litong-lito, Nakalimutan na ang mga isinaulo! Sa karerepaso, halos magkandaloko-loko, Gayunpaman, bagsak pa rin sa bandang dulo. Kung pagbubutihin ang paksa nang todo, Susupilin ng guro ang pasikat at pabibo! Kapag masayahin, ituturing na makulit, Ang nagpapakatotoo ay hinahagupit. Tingin nila, gaganahan kung pag-iinitan, Hindi nila alam—humihina ang kalooban! Ang kumpiyansa'y nagiging alinlangan, Kung hindi sumunod, ang laki ng kasalanan! Binabatay ang grado sa pagiging perpekto, Mali kung bawat salita'y hindi eksakto. Tuwing talakayan, tumpak dapat sa inulat, Kung 'di sasagot agad, ituturing kang kulelat. Pagmumukhain pang ikaw ang hangal, Eh, 'di naman sila malinaw mangaral. Gagamitan ka ng malalalim na salita, Eh, 'di naman lahat kabisado ang paksa. Hindi na alam kung anong dapat tanungin, Kung magkamali ng sabi, kakastiguhin! Hindi isinasaalang-alang ang damdamin, Basta't aburidong makatapos ng aralin. "Makinig ka kasi!" wika ng guro, Palaging aburido, mainitin ang ulo! Mahilig mamintas! Ang tindi magsalita, Kaya sa pangarap, wala na kaming gana... Kung mag-isip sana ang sistemang pamantasan: 'Wag na tayong bumalik sa gawi ng nakaraan! Alalahaning naging estudyante ka rin naman, At ang mga mag-aaral ay may karapatan. Hindi lamang ang nakapagtapos ang aasenso. Tandaan! May kanya-kanyang uri ng talino. Damayan ang mga nahihirapan sa kolehiyo; Nasa kamay mo ang kinabukasan ng tao. Isulong ang karampatan at kaayusan; Dapat magbigayan at maggalangan Kapwa ikaw at iyong mga mag-aaral— Dahil ang pagtuturo'y trabahong marangal. Karaingan sa Silid-Aralan Poem Written by: Ace Jacus May 21, 10:27:37 PM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] Alam mo, napaka-ano mo pala; Ikaw 'yung tipong ano—sobra! Ina-ano mo ang lahat ng bagay, Kaya tuloy, umano na ang lagay. Kung bigyan mo lang ako ng ano, 'Di masusukli ang ano mo sa'kin. Umpisa pa ay pina-ano mo lang ako, Tuloy—anu-anong gustong gawin! Naa-ano talaga ako sa atin, 'Di kita ma-anong umamin, 'Wag mo nang i-ano, alam ko na— Ina-ano mo, dahil nahiya ka! Kaso, bigla ka na lang naa-ano, Napapansin ang kung anu-ano! 'Yan naman ang ayaw ko, pero... Ako na lang magpapababa sa'yo! Bakit pa nakipag-ano noon? Napa-ano na tuloy tayo ngayon! Ikaw sa lahat ng oras ay uma-ano, Kaya kung ano na'ng tingin sa'yo! Ang sarap talaga noong nag-anuhan, Hinding-hindi ko iyon malilimutan; Karanasang paminsan-minsan lamang, Dahil napakamahal sa ganoong kainan. Kaka-ano sa 'di kaganda-ganda, Baka kaya 'yung ano ko'y wala na! Hindi ko naman iyon sinasadya, Kailangan, limitado kasi ang benta! Iparamdam sa'kin kung anong meron, Ta's mahaharap ko anumang hamon. Kung tumbasin lang sa iilang salita, 'Di rin ma-ano ang likas mong ganda! Hindi naman mahalaga kung ano, Kundi kung paano, at kung kanino. Basta't kasama kita sa anu-ano, Hindi na alintana ang ibang tao! Title: Ano Ko Sa'yo Poem Written by: Ace Jacus May 21, 10:26:23 PM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] Minsan, hindi na ang nagtuturo ang mali, Kundi ang nakaupo sa likod na mga lalaki, Seryoso na nga, magiging pang impertinente, Talagang mga bastos itong estudyante! Hahayaan na lang—hindi na lang ipapahiya; Nananamantala kayong mga astang bata! Ang babait na nga, inaabuso mo pa, tol? Pero kung galit—sa tropa nagmaktol?! Mapagbigay sa grado, hindi nagkulang, Kaso, baliwala! Palaging lutang at pabalang... Pare, kung ikaw ba'y walang pagpapahalaga, Huwag mo na lang sanang idamay ang iba! Kung ilagay ka sa grupo, aasa na lang sa A.I., Walang ambag! Kalokohan lang at tambay! Nanggugulo pa sa inaasikasong aralin, Pabuhat at pabigat pa sa may suliranin. Kabulastugan ang tangi mong kaalaman, Hungkag ang utak at salat sa karunungan! Tawagin ka para sumagot: wala kang alam. Sayang ang oras! Kokopya na lang sa eksam! 'Kala mo astig ka sa angas mong mamilosopo? Pwes, tuloy mo lang! Magpakasasa ka sa dulo! Tong-its pa! Hithit pa! Laklak pa! Bisyo pa— Pag-abot mo'ng impiyerno, paalam ka muna! Mabuting magsisi't huminahon kahit sandali, Iwasang magpaalam na umihi, 'tas saka uuwi— Pagdating ng puntong mag-isa ka na sa mundo, Diyos huwag po sanang maaga kang ma-dedo! [Baguio/Benguet] PROS AND CONS NG LRH At since nag sisilabasan na ang infos/opinions ng mga interns ng [Baguio/Benguet] dorms, ito lang masasabi ko as an LRH girlie. PROS: -Super lapit sa school syempre. Kapag may naiwan ka na gamit, isang takbuhan lang yon -Ayaw gumastos ng lunch? Balik lang agad sa dorm -SUPER SARAP NG MGA ULAM🥰 -Never malalate unless late ka magising -Laundry services -Friendly assistant matrons and staffs. YIEEE SHOUT OUT SAINYO ATES CONS: -Study periods everyday except Saturday and Sunday nights and if walang pasok kinaumagahan. OSP (Optional Study Period) are for DL’s only, mag attendance lang sila and they’re good to go, so I guess magaan naman onti. -Violations=warnings=non-renewal (bawal na magstay sa dorm) -7 pm curfew -Maingay ibang rooms and floors -Nawawala food sa ref and mga gamit sa mga tables That’s all. I get it naman na rules should be followed, pero wala po bang mas magaan? And I’m not saying po na dapat tanggalin study period, nakakatamad lang po kaso bumangon kapag nakaidlip na after dinner huhu. May 21, 9:36:34 PM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] This is a confession- not to someone, but to express myself. Incoming freshman, BSMLS Hindi ko alam kung saan ako magsisimula, but lately, I’ve been feeling anxious - and honestly, it’s because I’m not really sure if I truly want the program I chose. Una palang, I already had this feeling: hindi ako sigurado sa daan na pinili kong tahakin. Ako yung tipo na ‘go with the flow’. Never akong nagkaroon ng solid, long-term plan. I used to be okay with that. It never really bothered me - until now. I know iba ang college. Bawat desisyon na gagawin ay may kaakibat na bigat. And that’s when the doubt started creeping in: Ito ba talaga gusto ko? Kakayanin ko kaya ‘to? I came from STEM strand, and I survived it with flying colors. With high honors and other extracurricular awards. I gave it my best, and I did well. I think. But the truth is I don’t really have the passion for math and science. I worked hard, yes, but not because I loved the subjects - I pushed through because I had to. Now that papalapit na ang college, I keep asking myself: Can I learn to love the path I’ve chosen? Or sadyang pinipilit ko lang ang sarili kong mahalin ang bagay na hindi naman talaga daat para sakin? May mga araw na parang okay lang, pero may mga gabi rin na pinagdududahan ko ang aking sarili. I still don’t have all the answers. Hindi ko alam kung saan ako dadalhin ng agos ng buhay. But I’m hoping that somehow, along the way, I’ll find clarity - and maybe even grow to love the journey I’m on, even if it didn’t start with certainty. May 20, 10:31:30 PM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] LADIES [Baguio/Benguet] RESIDENCE HALL IS A NO NO. -medyo rant pero puno ng puot : sana maipost para aware ibang students sa gantong ganap sa loob ng dorm na to. Huwag niyo na subuking pumasok o tumira dito, dagdag breakdown lang sainyo, napakahigpit na nga lang dipa maayos mga rules. Mistake ng isa, suffer ang lahat? Wifi laging ngarag mas malakas pa hotspot. May dumi sa cr na hindi mo naman kalat, paglilinisin kayo lahat as if LHAT talaga eh naglilinis, yung iba nga nandon lang nanonood sa tabi imbes na mag brush ng bowl. Nataon lang na may school activity preparation kaya lumagpas lang ilang minutes sa 7pm curfew, suspended na agad ng 1 week? Hindi makakuha ng leave pass kasi nga sinuspend niyo, eh kailangan ko magpractice sa simbahan namin tas mamamark curfew? Ang unfair huh? Tapos pag nakapag violate pa next term, even unintentionally, palalayasin na dito sa dorm? Paano nalang yung mga students na dito pang gusto patirahin ng parents? Buti sana if yung mga curfew namin, all about gala at night outs lang. Hindi naman. Nacurfew lang dahil nga sinuspend niyo dahil sa isang gabi at ilang minutong lumampas lang ng 7pm kasi nga may school activity and unexpected na matatapos yun ng gabi, hindi makakuha ng leave pass. Nacucurfew dahil need magpractice sa simbahan, yes binigyan niyo ng time na 8:30 dapat nakauwi na pero di parin tapos kasi nga lahat ng nasa team more on students kaya minsan nakakapagstart na ng 7pm ang practice, violation agad. Nagsusubmit ng letters pag hindi makakaattend ng study period kasi nga may sakit or may need asikasuhin na hindi pwedeng gawin sa study hall kasi need magvideo or whatso. Para na rin hindi makaistorbo sa ibang students sa study hall. Tapos imamark niyo lang na absent? Hahaha! Pinalampas na nga namin yung "bawal ang gunting dito sa dorm or any matalim", tinawanan na nga lang namin yan kasi parang sira, student bawal ang gunting? May kitchen bawal ang knife? — Hirap ipagtanggol ng dorm na to. Kaya if ikaw nasisilaw ka magstay dito? Nah, baby. Wag mo na subukan. Kasi ako? Kung hindi lang gusto ng magulang ko na dito ako magstay? Hindi nako tutuloy dito. Or maybe, since dito unang option ko kasi nga sabi nila "best ang school dorm", matagal na akong lumayas/gumawa ng mga INTENTIONAL violations para mapalayas dito. :::Bedspacer na parang nakahiga sa kabaong kada matutulog sa sobrang ikip ng higaan. May 18, 8:18:14 PM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] Shouldn’t a Teacher... Teach? We’re Tired of the “Spoon-Fed” Excuse. We Deserve Real Educators. Hi, just wanted to know what are your thoughts on this. I hope you guys make time to read this. For our *** *** class, I had high hopes. On the first day of the second semester, during our course orientation, I found the instructor funny and cool. I was genuinely excited to learn more about the subject because it felt incredibly relevant to my program and personal interests. But now that the semester is ending, I can confidently and honestly say that not once did she actually teach or facilitate a meaningful discussion in class. I’m not even exaggerating. At one point, she even mentioned having a heavy workload, and said it as if to gain sympathy. But still, no change. Her typical routine consists of flashing a PowerPoint presentation, staying completely silent for 30 seconds to 2 minutes so we can copy the content, then moving on to the next slide. If there’s no presentation, she reads from her notes and expects us to write everything down at lightning speed. Every few seconds, students plead, “Ma’am, ang bilis po!” or “Ma’am, paulit po pleasee”, but she never acknowledges them. Not even once. She said we should learn how to properly take notes. At one point, before prelims began, she asked students she had already handled to leave the room so she could speak privately to the new ones. She asked what we thought about her teaching. One of my classmates respectfully shared that she learns better through discussions than by copying dictated notes. Did anything change after that? Sadly, no. Instead, she continued to randomly call on students to explain terms and concepts, without ever explaining them herself. Like?! Isn’t that the role of an instructor? What makes it worse is that many students, especially those she previously handled, have already complained. Her only justification was that we shouldn’t be “spoon-fed” and should learn on our own. But isn’t that the very essence of education? Instruction comes first. Then students internalize, reflect, and apply. Self-directed learning should be encouraged, but never at the expense of actual teaching. I had no idea whether anyone formally reported her. I would have done so myself if I knew that the process to hold instructors accountable, or at least push for improvement, was simple and effective. It seems incredibly difficult to initiate any real action. That realization was disheartening. It left me feeling powerless and frustrated, knowing that many students were being deprived of the education they deserve. When midterm grades were released, nearly half the class failed. And for finals? All we did was submit a video project. Those who failed midterms were asked to “recover” by doing classroom presentations. So for several days, we sat through the same topics being presented over and over and over and over again. Topics that the instructor should have taught in the first place. It felt like the entire class was doing her job. Don’t even get me started on her exams. It completely misaligned with the nature of the subject. Instead of thoughtful, reflective questions that encourage critical thinking, she gives fill-in-the-blank tests, expecting students to memorize entire paragraphs word-for-word, like it’s a math exam with only one correct answer. It’s like asking, “What’s a synonym for ‘good’?” and she only accepts “nice” while rejecting equally valid answers like “excellent,” “splendid,” or “satisfactory.” This course is supposed to celebrate diverse thinking and perspectives, yet her teaching style does the opposite. And to make matters worse, it’s clear she plays favorites. Some students are treated more kindly, while others are dismissed or ignored. My enthusiasm for this subject, which I once looked forward to, has been completely diminished. Learning should be engaging, eye-opening, and empowering. Instead, we were left to teach ourselves while she stood back and watched. We’re Not Asking for Much May 18, 6:29:11 PM HKT

I came across a secondhand iPad on Facebook Marketplace. I wasn't planning to buy one at first, since it was out of my budget, but this deal seemed reasonable. I did everything I could to verify the seller; checked her ID, the rider’s plate number, and trusted her word. Sadly, after sending the money, she disappeared. The device never arrived. Ilang beses na ako nag regret na pumasok ako sa school na to tapos malalaman kong ganto lang din pala yung sistema. Kaya oo, panget talaga sistema. HOWEVER... However, di ako agree na jinujustify ng circumstances natin ang pagccheat. Di lang naman kasi one-sided problem yung kinakaharap ng CE eh. Part din ng problem yung ibang mga estudyante at ang nasa sentro neto lahat ay yung sobrang rampant na cheating issues. Bawat major exam ko nalang yata mula prelims, may nahuhuling nagccheat sa room ko. Di lang yon, ang dami ding nababalitaan na nagchecheat sa determinant subjects. Yung mga dating maluluwag at mababait na instructors natin, naghihigpit na din ngayon. Naniniwala akong merong mga bagay na mababago natin at hindi natin mababago. Yung mismong admin, ewan ko parang wala sa control natin yan eh. Di naman sila makikinig sa gusto natin kung ayaw nila tayong pakinggan. Pero yung actions natin bilang student body ang isa sa nakaka impluwensya sa mga desisyon na ginagawa nila. Pag nakikita nilang maraming nagccheat, edi mas lalo lang tayong papahirapan. Pwede naman kasing mag sumikap at mag aral, despite the circumstances. Gawin parin sana kung anong makakaya kesa i compromise natin yung mga moral values natin. Kung sa pagaaral palang eh puro pandaraya na iniisip, paano pa sa trabaho? Kita mo board exam passing rate natin na basura pa sa basura? Paano tataas yon kung puro pandaraya nalang pinapa iral natin? Ang dami kong kilalang cheaters sa batch ko, at sa totoo lang nakakasama na ng loob eh. Yung iba, may academic incentive pang nakukuha eh puro pandaraya at pangongodigo lang ginagawa. Nakakahiya kaya? Katulad ng sabi ko, agree ako mostly sa mga sinabi mo. I just dont tolerate cheaters. May 18, 2:43:33 PM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] Hi everyone. This is actually my first time doing something like this—sending a confession to an FB group. I've been going back and forth about whether I should share this or not. It's something that's been on my mind for years—yes, literal years. I’ve just always felt so conflicted. Taking up Architecture was a big gamble for me. Not just because it’s a difficult course with no guaranteed income after graduation, but because of my personal circumstances. I’m the first in our small family to reach college. I’m also the panganay, and I had no one to guide me on how college life works—especially here at [Baguio/Benguet]. Everything I have now is from pure grit and stubborn effort. I didn’t come from a family with a firm waiting for me. I didn’t grow up surrounded by titos or cousins who are architects who could help when I was struggling to understand something. I entered this course with almost nothing—barely any materials—until I decided to work graveyard from Mondays through Sundays as a freelancer to be able to afford them. My parents cover my tuition, and I’m deeply thankful, but I know it’s a little heavy on them too. There were even times when some professors (yes, including that one kalbo na prof in Arki) told me maybe Architecture just wasn’t for me. And coming from someone in the field, it was a honestly a little upsetting. But I’d like to believe my mental fortitude is strong—so I shrugged it off. Dedma sa bashers. I’ve always known this path would be hard. But what made it even harder was the alienating air in the [DEPARTMENT]. As an irregular student, I often felt like I didn’t belong. There’s a subtle—sometimes not-so-subtle—divide between regular and irregular students. I’ve been intentionally excluded or overlooked during group projects, especially when grouped with block sections. I’ve clashed with a few students who misread my silence in group discussions as being uncooperative, when in fact, I already completed my share of work even before she asked for it. It became clear to me that there’s little to no sense of community in the Architecture department at [Baguio/Benguet]. And when both professors and peers make you feel like you were wrong to pursue this path, you begin to question yourself talaga. I realized—maybe it’s not Architecture I need to give up on...�Maybe it’s [Baguio/Benguet]. It’s bittersweet. I’ve had fun. I’ve made good friends and I’ve met amazing people. I won’t, can’t, and don’t want to give up on my dream of becoming a licensed architect someday. Because this dream isn’t just mine—�I’m carrying my family’s dream too. Especially my dad’s. He never had the chance to go to college, but deep down, he admitted he would’ve wanted to become an architect himself. So I’m walking this road not just for me, but for him and for all the sacrifices my family has made. It’s a little heavy on the heart, but I know this choice is leading me somewhere better. Somewhere where I can truly grow, belong, and keep chasing this dream. God is faithful, always. To all irregular arki students like me out there, or irregular students in general, I read a great quote somewhere that goes like this, “What a privilege it is to be tired in the pursuit of a challenge of your own choosing” Mapapagod pero ‘di susuko. ’Til we meet again, [Baguio/Benguet]. [Baguio/Benguet] [Storytime: The Weight of Two Lines] We became a couple. Every time we met, something happened between us. At first, we were careful. We used protection. There was fear, there were questions. But as time went by, he became too comfortable. And I stayed quiet. Then one day, no more protection. No more fear. Every time he came inside me, he whispered promises sweeter than safety. Then one day... my period didn’t come. Time froze. The world blurred. There I was, sitting on cold tiles, holding a dream that turned into a ghost. Two lines. Two strokes. Two words in my mind: "It’s over." I told him— not for pity, but hoping he’d hold my hand in the storm we created. But what he said... cut deeper than silence. It felt like a knife wrapped in calm. No guilt. No pause. Just cold words: "That’s not my problem." "I can’t handle that." "I have a future. I’m a Dean Lister. I have dreams." He said it like I was nothing. Like I didn’t matter. Like the life growing inside me was a burden he had no time for. In that moment, I wasn’t his girlfriend. I wasn’t someone he loved. I was just a mistake he wanted to erase. Since that day, I’ve felt lost. I can’t eat. I can’t sleep. My body is tired, but my heart is even more tired. And every night, I ask myself: Does he still think of me? I saw him once. [Seminary] He was laughing with his friends, eating like nothing happened. While I stood there, holding coins in my hand and tears in my eyes. They looked at me— but no one asked if I was okay. No one cared. Now, I don’t know if I still need to study at [Baguio/Benguet]. I don’t know if I still want to live or stay. Sometimes, I just want to disappear— to leave my friends, my family... even myself. But deep inside, a small voice tells me: “This is not the end. You still matter. You are not the shame he left behind. You are a mother now. You are strong.” And if you ever hear this, not as your secret, not as your regret, but as the girl who loved you completely, and now carries the life you walked away from... I hope you remember: You left something behind. Not just a 👶🏻. But a heart still learning how to heal from the silence you gave me. May 17, 7:24:04 PM HKT

Some of us have classes from 7 AM to 7 PM buong araw kaming ginagapang tapos after that, saka pa lang makakaaral... for four f***ing exams the next day. Seryoso? Anong klaseng torture ‘to? And also bakit nga ba may mga estudyanteng nandadaya? Eh kasi wala na silang oras mag-review. Sobrang compressed ng schedule, halos wala nang pahinga, tapos magtataka kayo kung bakit may nahuhuling nandadaya? Tangina, galit na galit pa kayo kapag may nahuli. Ayaw din naman ng mga estudyante bumagsak, pero wala na silang choice minsan. Kaya nila nagagawa yon out of desperation, hindi dahil gusto nila. Mag-isip-isip naman kayo. Hindi lahat ng bagay masosolusyonan sa parusa minsan, kailangan niyong tanungin sarili niyo kung bakit umaabot sa ganon. We’re not asking for the moon. Ang hinihingi lang namin ay konting consideration. Putangina, kahit one week man lang sana before ang labas ng exam schedule. Para makapaghanda kami, makapag-ayos ng oras, at kahit papano, mabawasan yung stress na binubuno namin araw-araw. Nakakadrain. Nakaka depress. Ang hirap na nga ng course, tapos ganito pa sistema niyo. Sana naman, makinig kayo sa mga estudyante hindi lang yung paulit-ulit na wala namang pagbabago. Fix this shit. Para sa kapakanan naming lahat. May 17, 7:01:15 PM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] It frustrates me how so many young Filipinos, especially Gen Zs, have this mindset of “I’m not going to vote because no one deserves it” or “I won’t vote because the corrupt always win anyway.” Like seriously? Some don’t even bother to register because they’ve already given up on the system. Meron pa yung “di ako mag vovote kasi di naman ako directly apektado” Oh my, how selfish are you?? Paano yung mga taong nasa laylayan? This mindset is exactly why nothing changes. If we keep turning our backs on our right to vote, we’re handing power over to the very people we complain about. And if we all keep thinking like that, then who’s left to actually change the system? Voting is not just a right it’s our responsibility. It’s one of the few powers we actually have as citizens. And the truth is, there will never be a perfect candidate. No one is perfect. All of us, whether politicians or ordinary people, have our own flaws. But that doesn’t mean we should just give up. There are people out there who are genuinely trying to make a difference, you just have to do the research. We are a product of many mistakes, generations and generations of them, but that doesn’t mean we’re doomed to repeat them. We can learn. We should learn. Maybe that’s the issue: people say no one is deserving, but they don’t even take the time to find out who’s out there. With all the access we have to information, that’s just plain laziness. Our generation is literally more educated than the ones before us. We should know better than to vote for someone just because they’re famous or the only name we recognize. So please, stop romanticizing not voting like it’s some kind of rebellion. It’s not. It’s ignorance. It’s just giving up. And we can’t afford to give up. Not now. Not ever. Wake up, youth. Wake up, Philippines. May 15, 12:57:54 PM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] Rant/Story time: TL;DR: Ginamit ko yung last cubicle sa women’s CR sa 6th floor ng Perfecto kasi wala nang available and magsstart na quiz ko. Paglabas ko, a prof confronted me, pointed at the “faculty use” sign, and called me out kahit dalawang beses na akong nag sorry, she kept blocking and scolding me instead of just informing me calmly. - This happened sa women’s CR sa 6th floor ng Perfecto. For context, I’m fully aware na the last cubicle is reserved for faculty use, pero that time, wala na talagang ibang available and magsstart na yung quiz ko, so I had no choice but to be quick. Since wala namang faculty sa CR at that moment, I decided to use it. Paglabas ko, I saw a prof leaning sa may wall outside the cubicle, staring at me with her arms crossed. Bigla niya akong hinarang and said, “Nakikita mo ba ‘yan?” sabay turo sa sign that said “for faculty use.” I was honestly taken aback, but I didn’t want to argue lalo na with a prof, so I immediately said, “Sorry po, ma’am.” Akala ko tapos na, pero hinarang niya ulit ako and pointed to the other cubicles, saying, “Ayan oh, eto yung para sa inyong mga estudyante.” I apologized again, said “Sorry po,” and left. - This is my message to you Ma’am: I don’t know who you are and I’m not sure which department you’re from because if I did, I would’ve preferred to speak with you face-to-face. But since I wasn’t given the chance to explain myself properly earlier, I’m writing this message in hopes that it reaches you. First, I want to make it clear that I’m fully aware the last cubicle is reserved for faculty. I only used it because I was in a rush and, at that moment, there were no other available cubicles for students. By the time you arrived, some may have been free, but I was already inside and doing my best to be quick and mindful of the situation. What bothered me is not just how the situation was handled, but how you chose to speak to me. I apologized, not once, but twice, acknowledging my mistake and showing respect. Yet you continued to corner me and speak in a condescending, almost confrontational manner. Doubling down with an unnecessarily aggressive tone doesn’t reflect professionalism or basic courtesy. I’ve had similar situations before where professors handled it with kindness, even offering the faculty cubicle to students when needed. You, however, made a point to assert authority in a way that felt more personal than procedural. That kind of treatment is something I didn’t expect from someone in your position. Being in a position of authority doesn’t give you the right to talk down on others, especially students. If the roles were reversed, I doubt you would tolerate being spoken to the way you spoke to me. It feels like you’ve gotten too comfortable speaking down to people, and if no one has told you yet, maybe it’s time for some reflection. You teach at an institution that values compassion and respect, and I hope that’s something you strive to show in your interactions because it didn’t feel that way to me. I really hope this isn’t how you speak to other students, because that would be disappointing, both for them and for the school. Respect goes both ways. I gave it. I deserved it back. May 8, 6:29:26 PM HKT

We laud every Baguio youth who joined us in solidarity. For the people of Baguio, for the youth: NO TO POLITICAL DYNASTIES! The youth deserves better, the youth forwards genuine alternative politics of change. A powerful few have turned governance into a family affair, passing down positions from generation to generation—using every means to retain control. In the upcoming Elections, 71 out of 82 governors come from political families, and 113 of 149 city mayors belong to dynasties. Among 156 party-list groups, 40 are linked to political dynasties. In the city of Baguio, we must reject all attempts to establish political dynasties. Dynasties are a clear threat to our democracy. When power is concentrated in the hands of the elite, corruption, abuse, and impunity thrive. We call on the Filipino youth and the broader public: ZERO VOTES FOR POLITICAL DYNASTIES! We must resist this rotten political system and fight for a new kind of politics—genuine, people-centered, and rooted in real change.”

[Baguio/Benguet] Political engagement and campaign rallies are meant to be grand. That’s it. They are not your venues for academic tokenism, much less for clout chasing. When you join mobilizations without even understanding the ideological or policy platforms you supposedly support, you are not participating—you are reducing activism into performative activism. And when those expected to uphold political literacy—Comelec officers, POSAS officers, more so, political science students—parade political events as mere props for recitations and Instagram stories, it stops being praxis. It becomes pure posturing, and worse, a mockery of what true civic engagement demands. You are in a discipline that demands critical political engagement—not performative, not mimicry. To participate meaningfully is to wrestle with contradictions, to fight between conflicts and to provide synthesis in between. Kung tunay ang layunin, dapat totoo rin ang paninindigan. This is just a call to stop reducing grandiose acts to your performative actions. Stop prostituting activism for academic clout and personal branding. Do better, officers. Let your actions speak of principle, not of empty validation and your clout chasing principles. Tigilan niyo ang pagsakay sa mga kampanyang hindi niyo man lang sinikap unawain nang buo. Hindi kayo naiiba sa mga bulag na tagasunod ng disinformation machinery ng DDS o ng Marcos propaganda ecosystem—sila, lumulutang sa echo chambers; kayo, naglulublob para makisabay. [REDACTED_NAME] 26, 7:39:57 PM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] The [Baguio/Benguet] KASAMA/SSC stands in solidarity with the studentry in the fight against harassment in all its forms. We are committed to fostering safe spaces for everyone and actively working towards social justice and harmony within our community. However, for the past few months, we are deeply concerned by the recent allegations of sexual harassment involving recognized students and even student officers within our university. Thus, consistent with the principles and mandates of the Safe Spaces Act (Republic Act ), the Code of Conduct under the Student Handbook of [Baguio/Benguet], and the KASAMA/SSC Constitution of and By-Laws of , the council firmly believes that every student has the right to a safe and nurturing environment, free from fear and intimidation, where they can pursue their academic and personal growth. Additionally, we stand firm that harassment of any kind is unacceptable and goes against the core values which [Baguio/Benguet] upholds. We will not tolerate such behavior and will actively support efforts to address these issues, ensuring that the voices of those affected are heard and that appropriate action is taken, in accordance with the provisions of the Safe Spaces Act and the Student Handbook. The council is dedicated to collaborating with the university administration, faculty, and student body to strengthen mechanisms for reporting harassment, providing support for victims, and implementing preventative measures. With this, in creating an effective avenue for grievance and suggestions, we are extending our helping hand to everyone who can give details and can actively participate in the investigation of these cases. You can submit your concern through the Louisian Grievance Box: directly message the [Baguio/Benguet] KASAMA/SSC Page, and/or submit an incident report directly to [Baguio/Benguet] Office of Student Affairs and Services. We urge the student body to utilize the provided avenues to report incidents and share information, as this can be crucial in ensuring a thorough and just resolution to these cases. Together, let us work towards building a Louisian community where respect, safety, and justice prevail. Makiisa sa pagsulong ng serbisyong ikaw ang sentro, dahil Luwisyano, . Labor Day: The legacies behind the marks of labor When the sun reaches the crest of its arc in the sky, laborers are the first to weather its blaze. Exhaustion and strain condense into droplets of sweat on their brows, evaporating quicker than cities are built. Each centavo earned leaves a mark: a callous on a migrant fisher’s hands from casting nets in foreign waters, a bruise on a housekeeper’s left cheek from where her employer struck her, and a bug bite on a farmer’s wrist from tilling ancestral land that corporations seek to acquire. Still, they push onward, for the thought of losing their jobs and letting their families go hungry is far more difficult to stomach. These daily trials and the workforce’s indispensable contributions to the nation are honored during the 1st of May every year. For more than a century, Labor Day has served as a salute to the backbone of our society, celebrated with paid days off or extra pay for those required to work. It commemorates years of struggle against injustice, recognizing the determination of those who fought for the privileges workers enjoy today. Labor Day traces its roots to the Filipino labor movement of the 1900s, led by the Union Obrera Democratica de Filipinas (UODF), the nation’s first labor organization. Founded by Isabelo de los Reyes in , the UODF staged rallies and strikes protesting the American government’s refusal to increase worker wages, culminating in de los Reyes’s arrest. His successor, [PROFESSOR_NAME], soon pushed for the colonial administration's recognition of May 1 as a national holiday. Their refusal led to the May 1, , rally, with over 100,000 workers mobilized by the UODF to protest imperialism and labor exploitation. Like his predecessor, this ended in [PROFESSOR_NAME]’s arrest—but the seeds were already sown. By , the Philippine Assembly passed a bill recognizing May 1 as a national holiday, and by , the first Labor Day was celebrated. Long after the historic May 1 protests, the struggles that plagued the workforce of the 1900s continue to echo into the 21st century, evolving to fit the present urban landscape. Minimum wage earners struggle against exploitative practices such as contractualization and “end-of-contract” or “endo” schemes. At the same time, they barely survive on wages that fail to keep up with inflation. As they toil within hazardous environments and substandard working conditions, they are also left vulnerable to heatstroke and other illnesses. PAGASA reports dangerous heat levels in more than 20 areas nationwide. Meanwhile, Overseas Filipino Workers (OFWs) sacrifice the warmth of home to fight battles on foreign soil, including overwork with little pay, abuse from their employers, recruitment scams, and working in the crossfire of war—all while grappling with homesickness, cultural alienation, language barriers, and mental health issues. This Labor Day, the shadows of injustice continue to lurk beneath hollow promises of progress. Along with it, the legacy of the protests endures. Labor leaders, unions, and workers carry the torches passed down by those before them, enduring waves of threats, suppression, and violence to fight for lasting reforms safeguarding their well-being and dignity. Each rallying cry on the streets propels them forward, giving them the strength to push back against the iron fists threatening to undermine their advocacies. Yet despite the various hardships they experience, they are always the first to step up, working diligently to breathe life into the nation’s economy and weathering storms to secure the well-being of their families. The pillars of our country, built by the worn hands of our workforce, are at risk of crumbling beneath our apathy and ignorance. Labor Day transcends annual celebrations to honor a movement that embodies the workforce's collective experiences, sacrifices, and hopes. Beyond Labor Day rituals and enjoying the privileges won by decades of resistance, it is up to us to amplify the voices of our workers and push for policies that actively uphold their rights and security. The calluses on the fisherman’s hands, the bruise painting the housekeeper’s cheek, and the roughened palms of the farmer are reminders to look past glorified depictions of Filipino resilience and recognize the issues beneath our workers' struggles. By Kimee Semougne Morales Artwork by Jayvee Manganip ___ Today, White & Blue, the official student publication of [Baguio/Benguet], stands with the Filipino workforce in celebrating Labor Day and recognizing its history. May this day serve as an opportunity to reflect on the daily sacrifices of our laborers and stand in solidarity with those who fight bravely to shape the system to protect their welfare. And as election day approaches, may we be reminded of the power we hold to shape the future of our compatriots. In honoring our laborers, may we cast our votes to protect their well-being, rights, and dignity.
happy labor day! ⚙️🛠️

[Baguio/Benguet] ELEVATOR EXPERIENCE Calling the attention of maintenance at [Baguio/Benguet] in the Perfecto Building. I often ride the elevator because my room is on the 7th floor. This isn't the first time I've had an incident like this in the elevator. My first encounter was on the first day of the second semester, when I was with my former classmates from the first semester. We got stuck for a few minutes, but I wasn’t too affected. However, today at around 11:00 AM, none of us expected to go through something like this. There were nine of us in the elevator which is under its 15-person capacity. we were even laughing and joking around, when suddenly the elevator dropped from the 4th floor to the 2nd floor as we were heading down to the 1st. Some of my classmates panicked including me. We didn't expect that. Their response was agonizingly slow. We hit the open button but nothing happened. Luckily, by the time we hit the emergency button and reached the 2nd floor, someone noticed us. A group of people rushed over and worked together trying to open the doors since the open button still wouldn’t register. They even had to break the door. It was incredibly traumatizing, especially because of how long it took for anyone to help. It was a life or death situation. Thankfully, no one was hurt, but the impact to me was so severe that I still can't get over from time to time. I just hope this never happens to anyone else. - 1st year psych student [REDACTED_NAME] 24, 6:25:00 PM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] Nakakainis lang sa CE kasi kung ano pa yung 4-5 units na subject, which is yung pinakamahihirap for a semester and pinakamaraming meetings in a week, sila pa yung sobrang matipid sa activities (seatworks, quizzes and/or other assessments + the term exam). I am currently enrolled in a subject that is notorious to be one of the hardest subjects sa CE. Dito nadadale yung karamihan. Maraming nagiging irreg because of this subject and even some of our instructors now, sa subject na ito din daw sila naging irreg. student noon (sa same instructor pa haha visiting prof kasi) (PS. No shame to irregular students). Maraming nangangarap maglatin honors pero napuputol ang pangarap just because of this specific subject. Ayaw ko man icompare ang bawat situation pero it's the reality of what's happening. May mali din siguro ang studyante pero I think it's also fair to say na may mali na talaga sa sistema kung ganito lagi nangyayari. Idk if it's the system of CE that's wrong or the system of this specific instructor na hindi pinapansin ng admin. Labas ko lang frustration ko kasi of all the subjects na kilalang kilala to have multiple failing students in a semester (20% or lower lang pumapasa sa isang klase), bakit itong subject pa yung ipinagkakait ng activities? Wala bang rule/protocol man lang about the minimum number of assessments a specific X-unit subject should have? Kaya ba tipid sa activities kasi tinatamad yung instructor? Is it because yun ang goal ng school, to have more failing students every sem? Halata na ngang may mali sa sistema pero bakit wala man lang ginagawa na action para mas mabawasan ang bumabagsak? Our instructor only gave us 1 quiz and 1 exam each for prelims and midterms. Mind you, it's a 4 unit subject. Wala kaming chance bumawi. He's even known to be selfish with partial points. Kung mali mo or kulang yung isang part sa solution kahit walang effect sa final answer, mali na lahat. Siguro tanggap pa namin yung kadamutan niya sa partial points pero ang unfair lang na wala man lang kami chance bumawi kung naibagsak namin yung first quiz, or worse, kung zero nakuha namin sa quiz 1. We had our first quiz today for finals and ang lungkot lang kasi ang mindset na naming magkakaklase is to give up after the quiz kasi nga baka wala nanaman susunod na quiz. Hindi naman sa tamad na tamad na kaming lumaban but it's because our circumstances with this prof and this subject gives us MORE REASONS to give up rather than have hope. A chance to make up for our failing scores is all we want😞. Send help sa CE :< [REDACTED_NAME] 24, 6:22:00 PM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] !!️HELP!!️ I found intimate videos and nudes of a very specific couple (that I'm not familiar with) in my boyfriend's phone. Calling out all the Louisian students there na LDR!!! Help your girlie out, I need your thoughts. Me: in an LDR with a Manileño, very understanding and "martyr" (as per my friends) but gets narrow-minded when stressed and under pressure. Him: Big 3 student, communicates a lot, has a lot of outlet for stress, green flag naman. Us (My POV): - Legal both side of the fam. - Very stable, daig pa 'yung calm after the storm sa [REDACTED_NAME] 4:39. - We're not perfect, but we're trying our best to be very understanding of each other's situations and circumstances, since LDR kami (hence, the "martyr" claims of my friends). - There's an enough trust between us that jealousy is not much of an issue; we're both not the jealous type, but we're not that insensitive to not feel threatened by an existence of an outside party. - We usually openly communicate stuff, but there are matters na I preferred to talk about personally (not virtually), which is why some of those matters take time to get resolved (this is one of those). - We don't have much rules in our relationship because we mainly built our relationship around trust and maturity. My (22F) boyfriend (23M) is an S-brand phone user and I used to be one (now I'm just an Android girlie). I know some secret hacks and stuff about S-brand and I shared all of them with him since he became an S-brand user, one of which is the "Secure Folder" feature of S-brand. I have full access to his phone whenever we meet, and last week (Holy Week, talk about the irony), while he's taking a bath, I used his phone since mine's low batt. I felt an urge to check his gallery in the SF expecting nothing from it and failed miserably. I found couple of videos of a specific couple (like, 'tong couple lang na 'to 'yung nasa SF gallery) doing the deed. At first, I was taken aback because WHY DOES HE HAVE SCANDALS ON HIS PHONE? We talked about this kind of stuff before and we're both against people sharing and downloading intimate videos without the consent of those involved in the video, so I was really baffled when I saw those. My second taught was MAYBE they're his friends and he got it from them directly, because I've heard stories of L-n students being wild af that sometimes they openly share intimate videos of them with their s/o or hook-up to their friends (nonetheless, I still think it's very inappropriate to watch it, even saving it). But as I scrolled through the SF, I got more and more confused, nervous, jealous, upset, lahat na ng negative, because I saw pictures (selfies, thirst traps, nudes) of the girl ('yung girl do'n sa videos). To save their scandal is one thing, but to find out na he also saved non-intimate ones like selfies is another. Parang nagka-tinnitus ako that time, my mind went blank and my sight went blurry. I couldn't move for a while because I was having a hard time making sense out of the things I saw. Seeing the videos had me thinking na maybe he's just THAT horny, but it's the selfies that I can't explain; they looked "saved" and not taken with his camera since the photos are bit grainy (constant). My thoughts were: 1. Is he cheating on me wih the girl? If yes, then why is there only 1 guy, that's not him, in the videos? 2. Does he know them personally? Who could they possibly be? 3. Is he trying to hide it from me? Why does he have those in his SF gallery when he knows damn well that I'm the one who taught him the feature? (if he's hiding it from me, then that's just dumb) My boyfriend's very physical. Since we're in an LDR, I decided (on my own) to let him enjoy his po**, because, in my mind, I can't always be there to satisfy his needs and I'm still not ready to satisfy his sexual needs. But seeing that there's a specific girl with intimate videos, nudes, and non-sexual selfies in his SF gallery made me feel threatened, like I'm losing him. When he came back after his bath, I was so confused and nervous that I almost threw his phone away from me as if I'm the one hiding something. I don't like this feeling and I don't like this situation at all. Honestly, I feel like I know what's really happening and I'm aware of what should be done, but, somehow, I'm still delulu over him, hoping na whatever's in my mind is not right. So help me have clarity and wisdom before I talk to him. - Magb-boards sa November girlie and takot magkaheartbreak during boards szn. [REDACTED_NAME] 24, 7:13:30 AM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] Truth be told, the results of the mock elections in campus don't mean shit. Unpopular opinion and harsh reality? Makabayan senate candidates aren't going to win. It's not even going to be close. If the main issue right now is to prevent the Duterte allies from getting into senate to have a better chance for her impeachment this July, we need to make sure winnable candidates who are anti-duterte get in the Magic 12, even if this means Marcos allies. And I hate having to admit this. Vote for Kiko, Vote for Bam. But they are really the only opposition candidates who have a real shot at winning. Vote for Castro, Arambulo, etc? You basically assure the victory of more PDP-Laban candidates. One would argue that staying true to their chosen candidates is important as you want and need to make a statement. But is it truly worth it if the candidate loses and has no impact to the government afterward? What is that statement going to do when Philippine politics in status quo revolves around Marcos vs Duterte, and the distant future is dependent on this Midterm election? Honestly, May 12 is going to be all about strategy and having a sense of urgency. If PDP-Laban wins, and we fail to impeach [REDACTED_NAME]. You just know that when she runs in and has a huge chance in winning, The Dutertes will have the Philippines under control for good. Don't let that happen. Be smart about your votes. The opposition will live to fight another day when the Dutertes are out of the question. I hate the idea of having to vote for some Pro-Marcos candidates, but if it means blocking more Pro-Duterte candidates from sitting in the senate, then I will have to do whats necessary to make that happen. I've hated the Marcoses and Dutertes ever since. But right now, this is all about choosing the lesser evil. It is also how "The enemy of my enemy is my friend". This election will dictate the future of the Philippines for decades to come. May the God's above us and the Universe have mercy on our beloved country. P.S: No to political dynasty in Baguio City! [Baguio/Benguet] To the students in the hallways, especially during dismissal times Pls po wag kayo mag kwentuhan na nakaharang sa gitna ng hallway po!! The hallway is already cramped enough with students waiting on both sides to enter the classrooms and stalling in the middle of the hallway will only make it more congested. Apart from that, pls pick up the pace kung paalis po kayo sa classrooms since may mga students and instructors na may klase pa sa room ninyo. You can just step aside in the hallway to talk to each other or just go to the floor lobby or more open areas so that the hallways will not be congested. We may all feel that we are all main characters to walk slowly in the middle of the hallway or even the middle of the stairs but have some consideration for those who have classes after you and those about to be late po. [Baguio/Benguet] hello! girlie na psych student po. di to bro advice but more of girl advice. try nio po tignan if comfortable siya sayo before striking up a convo, a lot of us girls kasi minsan pag di namin bet na maging kaibigan or acquaintance ung guy di po usually namin ieentertain masyado ung usapan. if comfortable nmn po siya sainyo, try nio po magtanong ng questions na pwede niyang pagkwentuhan ng backstory. some questions could be: "do u have scars? pano mo nakuha?" [Baguio/Benguet] This might seem odd to some, and I understand there will be people who’ll ask na bakit kailangan mo pang ipost? Hindi ba pwedeng i-private message na lang? I get that. But this isn’t about humiliating anyone, especially that we are both in SEA. To the SEA freshie, a first-year male student whom I’ve never met personally and never had any real interaction with on campus, this message is for you. I, being your senior, have been hearing from others that you've been openly saying we’re together. You've been calling me your boyfriend, talking as if we’re in a relationship, when the truth is, we are not. We never had a real conversation. We don’t chat, we don’t hang out, and we never had any agreement or moment that could even be mistaken for something romantic. I want to start by saying this clearly. I’m not angry that you may have liked me. It’s not a crime to admire someone, to feel something, or even to wish for a connection. We are human. We feel. And I don’t want to make you feel ashamed of that. But what I cannot stay silent about is how you handled those feelings. Sharing things that aren’t true, telling people I’m your boyfriend, and building a story that involves me without my consent, those are not okay. It’s one thing to have feelings, but it’s another to involve someone else in your personal narrative without their knowledge or permission. Because of what you’ve said, someone very important to me was affected. I’ve been courting this girl for years. I’ve been consistent, sincere, and hopeful that someday she will see how genuine I’ve been. But when she heard that I was already taken, but when she believed the story that you’ve been sharing, she gave me a bitter no. Not because I did anything wrong, but because she believed a lie. A lie I had no part in creating. Do you know how that feels? To lose someone you’ve been holding onto for so long, because of something you didn’t even do? I can’t explain the level of pain and frustration that came with that. And that’s why I needed to speak up. This is something that broke a connection I’ve spent years nurturing. Again, I want to make it clear. I’m not here to shame you. I’m not here to mock your feelings. I understand what it’s like to admire someone from afar, to hope for something more. But I hope you also understand that there are proper ways to deal with those feelings. Creating false stories, telling people we are together, and allowing those lies to spread, those choices hurt people. They hurt me. They hurt her. And maybe, if you take a moment to reflect, they hurt you too. I’m not asking for an apology. I’m not expecting anything. But I am hoping that this helps you realize the importance of truth, boundaries, and respect. We can’t control who we like, but we can control how we act on those feelings. l So please, stop telling people we’re in a relationship. Stop calling me your boyfriend. And if there’s anything you need to work through, I hope you find healthier, kinder ways to do that. Not just for me, but for yourself and the people around you. I know that your feelings are valid, but they don’t give us the right to write stories in other people’s names. [REDACTED_NAME] 22, 4:27:56 PM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] Grief is like a houseguest you can't bring yourself to stop inviting over. “It's cold outside; just spend the night.” “It's much too late; just stay.” “Don't worry, the couch is free.” So it stays and makes itself comfortable on your couch until morning comes. The new day arrives, and you catch sight of them sleeping soundly on your couch. Your gut turns, and you feel bad sending them off with an empty stomach. So you heat up the pan, crack an egg or two, and make them a cup of coffee. They wake up, enjoy their breakfast, and prolong the conversations until your clock reads noon. Your gut turns at unease again. You can’t let them go without a meal. So you take the meat out of the freezer and let it thaw. A hearty meal for both of you to share. Soon the afternoon weighs heavy upon the both of you, and you fall asleep together on the couch. You wake up when the sun no longer colors in the sky, and once again, gnawing unease fills your gut. They wake up at the sound of your movements, and they say, “Oh, look how late it is already.” You nod, and you reply, “Just stay. It's too late, anyway. It's not safe for you to walk at night. Besides, it's cold, and my couch is warm and free. So, stay.” [REDACTED_NAME] 21, 5:19:30 AM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] I didn't grow up in a family where politics wasn't a big deal for us, and I just wanted to get others' opinions about these campaign posters posted all around. Di lang sa Baguio, pero pati sa Manila. Ang dugyot lang tignan kasi kung saan saan lang nila nilalagay, tapos ang daming waste pagkatapos ng elections. I know it's to show support sa mga iboboto nila, o kaya pang-PR para makita sila, pero all I can think of is the amount of money wasted to print these posters and tarpaulins, resources wasted tapos itatapon lang after a few months. The more nakikita ko mga mukha nila, the more na mas gusto sila kalimutan. Paano natin sila iboboto kung ngayon nawala pa sila sa pwesto di na nila macontrol ang supporters nila sa pag produce and distribute ng tons of waste materials. Obviously, no hate or bias sa mga candidates, pero there has to be better and environmentally safe ways to publicize your intention to run. This shit covers entire mountains. [REDACTED_NAME] 20, 12:52:15 PM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] Ah, I never imagined me being this low to vent my anger. It was like, “Whatever I did, I did it for love.” wala akong balak mag entry not until that girl forwarded our message just because she couldn't handle the truth, making it look like I was making a mess and ruining them, not until, they flipped the story, telling me that I’m the one who cheated. I almost died twice, sacrificed my life, and the moment I left ng madaling araw because I really can't handle the disrespect anymore. This guy is studying at [Baguio/Benguet]; I'm also studying sa [Baguio/Benguet]. Fast forward, we’ve been together for 1 year and exactly ended in our 1 year. Funny right? It was like a free trial pero 1 year lang. Typical story: He cheated. I think I can accept that and move on in my life, but before he cheated, we lived under the same roof, nag live in kami HAHAHA embarrassing, and unfortunately, we did naka buo and that baby died. After all those things, I think he was the love of my life because he never left and still loves me. (That's what I felt, at least.) Not until I found out that he’s cheating na pala. Like I said, nag live in kami, hindi pa siya ganon ka bilis naka lipat agad. Mind that he’s communicating with his girlfriend ngayon sa discord while I was there sa lang sa baba ng bed? That's a huge disrespect. I loved him. It's funny that I confronted the girl; she knew and continued to label everything as “unconditional love.” It's partly my fault, too, because I let them treat me that way, but the moment I knew that girly was forwarding my message sa mama ng ex ko, looking like I was ruining their relationship while I just told her the truth. Wow, the audacity of a side chick that became a main and having a supported mother who tolerated his son, telling me that I should move on, and matagal na yon. This makes me trigger and make this entry. You made it all look like it was all me na hindi lang maka move on; it was all just me that can't accept what happened, and I'm just ruining their so-called relationship. It really is easy to flip the story, making it look like I'm the one who cheated. I never did. You all want me to respect the side piece? No, she knew. I blame almost everything on my ex? Ofc, I sacrificed my life and my everything just for me to be treated as nothing and used me. It may be just a year, but try to put yourselves in my shoes; it may be a mistake, and I can take accountability for that. I learned my lesson. But that “ang tagal na non, mag move kana” “blina blame mo lahat kay -” invalidates me. That something happened, that has a life involved. Tell me, sinong makaka move on ng fully agad don? It's funny that you made me a stalker just because I'm finding out everything; it's funny that you're worshiping God but failed to have empathy and tolerate such. If I see something here, I will not be nice; you know what you did, the threats and such. And for you girly studying at [Metro Manila], you knew. I know that you are a top student. Now stop being naive and stop making yourself a victim. You said that whatever happened, you deserve it for what you did. And I said what I said, I love how na ikaw na pinag c cheatan ngayon, now don't make yourself a victim, acting like aping aping ka. Gosh. You don't get to tell me to move on while being biased based on what you just want to hear. I am a good person and have every right to be mad. I always make sure that I have a point and proof when I'm confronting someone. What a shame that I loved someone, but it ruined me. [REDACTED_NAME] 20, 12:11:41 PM HKT

[Baguio/Benguet] There was this instructor sa isang drafting subject ko. He marked something sa plate ko, may binilugan in red ink, and I was confused kasi nung tinanong ko kung ano yun, ang sabi lang "basta may kulang, alam mo dapat yan." I was certain na walang kulang sa part na yun kasi I specifically asked about that part of the design before submission. So I tried politely asking again kasi I have no idea talaga and I really did my research. So since ayaw niyang sabihin, I had no choice but to just resubmit it as is, I couldn't deal with it at the moment dahil hell week and hindi naman siya exam plate. Malalaman ko din lang ibig sabihin when it's returned. Or so I thought... Nung binalik yung plate, this instructor asked me kung anong tinanggal ko sa binilugan niya. I told him "wala po, sabi niyo po may kulang" and I explained everything. Then he said "wala namang kulang, alam mo naman palang kulang edi dapat sinabi mo" THEN BINAWASAN NG 5 pts YUNG SCORE KO EH 30 pts TOTAL LANG YUNG PLATE?!? WTFGAFSGSHAFAGDGSFSFSFSG?!?! Hindi ko na ipinakausap cause I was just too exhausted and it looked like wala din siya sa mood to hear students out. Wala na din akong gana because historically super sarcastic din niya kapag may nagtatanong and he won't answer straightly kaya mas nakakalito lang pag nagtatanong. But this is a lesson to instructors. Please, your title is literally "instructor," please just answer kapag may tanong. Maybe it's obvious to you as an instructor, but some students aren't as advanced or skilled. And what's the point of marking something na hindi naman pala kulang/ mali? It wasn't even an exam, kaya sana tinuro mo nalang sana kung ano yung mali noh? [REDACTED_NAME] 20, 9:36:08 AM HKT

Na ginagawa nya lahat lahat ng efforts just to save their relationship. Gets?) This makes me very sad fr. Many women can endure all the pain and heartbreak for a man. In those tough times, when nothing seems clear, a woman will still stay because she loves and believes in him. Despite all the red flags and disrespect, she hoped he would change for the betterment of their relationship. But for some men, when they experience those down phases in the relationship, how can they easily decide to let go of all of it in just one snap? All the connections they've built together, all the memories, all their future plans, all the promises? It's supposed to be a relationship that ends with marriage. Of course, having those rough patches when you're in a long-term relationship is naturally part of it. It's supposed to be part of being committed to one another, right? Whatever happens, you stick together if you are really committed to one another. But if it's that easy for you to let it all go, does it only mean you're not fully committed to the relationship? How can someone invest so much time in a relationship if they're not committed to it for a lifetime?
